authors_text: Zhong Meng,Tongzhou Chen,Rohit Prabhavalkar,Yu Zhang,Gary Wang,Kartik Audhkhasi,Jesse Emond,Trevor Strohman,Bhuvana Ramabhadran,W. Ronny Huang,Ehsan Variani,Yinghui Huang,Pedro J. Moreno
title: Modular Hybrid Autoregressive Transducer
title_zh: 模块化混合自回归传感器
abstract: Text-only adaptation of a transducer model remains challenging for end-to-end speech recognition since the transducer has no clearly separated acoustic model (AM), language model (LM) or blank model. In this work, we propose a modular hybrid autoregressive transducer (MHAT) that has structurally separated label and blank decoders to predict label and blank distributions, respectively, along with a shared acoustic encoder. The encoder and label decoder outputs are directly projected to AM and internal LM scores and then added to compute label posteriors. We train MHAT with an internal LM loss and a HAT loss to ensure that its internal LM becomes a standalone neural LM that can be effectively adapted to text. Moreover, text adaptation of MHAT fosters a much better LM fusion than internal LM subtraction-based methods. On Google's large-scale production data, a multi-domain MHAT adapted with 100B sentences achieves relative WER reductions of up to 12.4% without LM fusion and 21.5% with LM fusion from 400K-hour trained HAT.
summary: Text-only adaptation of a transducer model remains challenging for end-to-end speech recognition since the transducer has no clearly separated acoustic model (AM), language model (LM) or blank model. In this work, we propose a modular hybrid autoregressive transducer (MHAT) that has structurally separated label and blank decoders to predict label and blank distributions, respectively, along with a shared acoustic encoder. The encoder and label decoder outputs are directly projected to AM and internal LM scores and then added to compute label posteriors. We train MHAT with an internal LM loss and a HAT loss to ensure that its internal LM becomes a standalone neural LM that can be effectively adapted to text. Moreover, text adaptation of MHAT fosters a much better LM fusion than internal LM subtraction-based methods. On Google's large-scale production data, a multi-domain MHAT adapted with 100B sentences achieves relative WER reductions of up to 12.4% without LM fusion and 21.5% with LM fusion from 400K-hour trained HAT.
abstract_zh: 由于传感器没有明确分离的声学模型 (AM)、语言模型 (LM) 或空白模型，因此传感器模型的纯文本适应对于端到端语音识别仍然具有挑战性。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种模块化混合自回归传感器（MHAT），它具有结构上分离的标签和空白解码器，以分别预测标签和空白分布，以及共享声学编码器。编码器和标签解码器输出直接投影到 AM 和内部 LM 分数，然后添加到计算标签后验。我们使用内部 LM 损失和 HAT 损失来训练 MHAT，以确保其内部 LM 成为可以有效适应文本的独立神经 LM。此外，与基于内部 LM 减法的方法相比，MHAT 的文本适应促进了更好的 LM 融合。在 Google 的大规模生产数据上，采用 100B 句子的多域 MHAT 在没有 LM 融合的情况下实现了高达 12.4% 的相对 WER 降低，在 40 万小时训练的 HAT 中，使用 LM 融合实现了 21.5% 的相对 WER 降低。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种模块化混合自回归转导器（MHAT），通过结构化分离标签和空白解码器，提升端到端语音识别的文本适应能力。
AI.motivation: 传统转导器模型缺乏明确的声学模型（AM）、语言模型（LM）和空白模型，导致文本适应效果不佳，限制了其在语音识别中的应用。
AI.method: MHAT采用共享声学编码器与分离的标签/空白解码器，通过内部LM损失和HAT损失联合训练，使内部LM可独立适配文本，并优化标签后验概率计算。
AI.result: 在Google大规模生产数据上，MHAT通过100B句子适配后，相对词错误率（WER）降低12.4%（无LM融合）至21.5%（有LM融合），优于基线HAT模型。
AI.conclusion: MHAT通过模块化设计和内部LM优化，显著提升了转导器的文本适应能力与语言模型融合效果，为端到端语音识别提供了新思路。

authors_text: Ruoyang Liu,Chenhan Wei,Yixiong Yang,Wenxun Wang,Huazhong Yang,Yongpan Liu
title: Block-Wise Dynamic-Precision Neural Network Training Acceleration via Online Quantization Sensitivity Analytics
title_zh: 通过在线量化灵敏度分析进行分块动态精度神经网络训练加速
comment: 7 pages, to be published in 28th Asia and South Pacific Design Automation Conference (ASP-DAC 2023)
comments: 7 pages, to be published in 28th Asia and South Pacific Design Automation Conference (ASP-DAC 2023)
abstract: Data quantization is an effective method to accelerate neural network training and reduce power consumption. However, it is challenging to perform low-bit quantized training: the conventional equal-precision quantization will lead to either high accuracy loss or limited bit-width reduction, while existing mixed-precision methods offer high compression potential but failed to perform accurate and efficient bit-width assignment. In this work, we propose DYNASTY, a block-wise dynamic-precision neural network training framework. DYNASTY provides accurate data sensitivity information through fast online analytics, and maintains stable training convergence with an adaptive bit-width map generator. Network training experiments on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet dataset are carried out, and compared to 8-bit quantization baseline, DYNASTY brings up to $5.1\times$ speedup and $4.7\times$ energy consumption reduction with no accuracy drop and negligible hardware overhead.
summary: Data quantization is an effective method to accelerate neural network training and reduce power consumption. However, it is challenging to perform low-bit quantized training: the conventional equal-precision quantization will lead to either high accuracy loss or limited bit-width reduction, while existing mixed-precision methods offer high compression potential but failed to perform accurate and efficient bit-width assignment. In this work, we propose DYNASTY, a block-wise dynamic-precision neural network training framework. DYNASTY provides accurate data sensitivity information through fast online analytics, and maintains stable training convergence with an adaptive bit-width map generator. Network training experiments on CIFAR-100 and ImageNet dataset are carried out, and compared to 8-bit quantization baseline, DYNASTY brings up to $5.1\times$ speedup and $4.7\times$ energy consumption reduction with no accuracy drop and negligible hardware overhead.
abstract_zh: 数据量化是加速神经网络训练、降低功耗的有效方法。然而，执行低比特量化训练具有挑战性：传统的等精度量化会导致高精度损失或有限的位宽减少，而现有的混合精度方法提供了高压缩潜力，但无法执行准确有效的位宽分配。在这项工作中，我们提出了 DYNASTY，一种分块动态精度神经网络训练框架。 DYNASTY 通过快速在线分析提供准确的数据敏感性信息，并通过自适应位宽图生成器保持稳定的训练收敛。在CIFAR-100和ImageNet数据集上进行了网络训练实验，与8位量化基线相比，DYNASTY带来了高达$5.1\times$的加速和$4.7\times$的能耗降低，且精度没有下降，硬件开销可以忽略不计。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于块级动态精度的神经网络训练加速框架，通过在线量化敏感性分析实现高效训练。
AI.motivation: 传统量化方法存在精度损失或压缩潜力有限的问题，而现有混合精度方法无法准确分配比特宽度，需解决低比特量化训练中精度与效率的矛盾。
AI.method: 设计DYNASTY框架，通过在线量化敏感性分析动态分配块级比特宽度，结合自适应映射生成器维持训练稳定性。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100和ImageNet上实现5.1倍加速与4.7倍能耗降低，精度无损失且硬件开销可忽略。
AI.conclusion: DYNASTY有效平衡了量化精度与训练效率，为神经网络加速提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 提出一种基于块级动态精度的神经网络训练加速框架，通过在线量化敏感性分析实现高效训练。
ai_content.motivation: 传统量化方法存在精度损失或压缩潜力有限的问题，而现有混合精度方法无法准确分配比特宽度，需解决低比特量化训练中精度与效率的矛盾。

authors_text: Jia Li,Ge Li,Zhuo Li,Zhi Jin,Xing Hu,Kechi Zhang,Zhiyi Fu
title: CodeEditor: Learning to Edit Source Code with Pre-trained Models
title_zh: CodeEditor：学习使用预训练模型编辑源代码
comment: Accepted by the ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology (TOSEM)
comments: Accepted by the ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology (TOSEM)
abstract: Developers often perform repetitive code editing activities for various reasons (e.g., code refactoring) during software development. Pre-trained code editing models have achieved the state-of-the-art (SOTA) results. Pre-trained models are first pre-trained with pre-training tasks and fine-tuned with the code editing task. Existing pre-training tasks mainly are code infilling tasks (e.g., masked language modeling), which are derived from the natural language processing field and are not designed for automatic code editing. This paper proposes a novel pre-training task specialized in code editing and presents an effective pre-trained code editing model named CodeEditor. Our pre-training task further improves the performance and generalization ability of code editing models. Specifically, we collect lots of real-world code snippets as the ground truth and use a powerful generator to rewrite them into mutated versions. Then, we pre-train our CodeEditor to edit mutated versions into the corresponding ground truth, to learn edit patterns. We conduct experiments on four code editing datasets and evaluate the pre-trained CodeEditor in three settings. (1) In the fine-tuning setting, we train the pre-trained CodeEditor with four datasets and evaluate it on the test data. CodeEditor outperforms the SOTA baselines by 15%, 25.5%, and 9.4% and 26.6% on four datasets. (2) In the few-shot setting, we train the pre-trained CodeEditor with limited data and evaluate it on the test data. CodeEditor substantially performs better than all baselines. (3) In the zero-shot setting, CodeEditor correctly edits 1,113 programs while the SOTA baselines can not work.

(2) In the few-shot setting, we train the pre-trained CodeEditor with limited data and evaluate it on the test data. CodeEditor substantially performs better than all baselines. (3) In the zero-shot setting, CodeEditor correctly edits 1,113 programs while the SOTA baselines can not work.
abstract_zh: 在软件开发过程中，开发人员经常出于各种原因（例如代码重构）执行重复的代码编辑活动。预训练的代码编辑模型已经达到了最先进的（SOTA）结果。预训练模型首先通过预训练任务进行预训练，并通过代码编辑任务进行微调。现有的预训练任务主要是代码填充任务（例如掩码语言建模），这些任务源自自然语言处理领域，并不是为自动代码编辑而设计的。本文提出了一种专门针对代码编辑的新型预训练任务，并提出了一种有效的预训练代码编辑模型，名为 CodeEditor。我们的预训练任务进一步提高了代码编辑模型的性能和泛化能力。具体来说，我们收集了大量现实世界的代码片段作为基本事实，并使用强大的生成器将它们重写为变异版本。然后，我们预训练代码编辑器将变异版本编辑为相应的基本事实，以学习编辑模式。我们在四个代码编辑数据集上进行实验，并在三种设置中评估预训练的 CodeEditor。 (1) 在微调设置中，我们使用四个数据集训练预训练的 CodeEditor，并在测试数据上对其进行评估。 CodeEditor 在四个数据集上的表现优于 SOTA 基线 15%、25.5%、9.4% 和 26.6%。 (2) 在少样本设置中，我们用有限的数据训练预训练的 CodeEditor 并在测试数据上对其进行评估。 CodeEditor 的性能明显优于所有基线。 (3) 在零样本设置下，CodeEditor 正确编辑了 1,113 个程序，而 SOTA 基线无法工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为CodeEditor的预训练代码编辑模型，通过设计专门的预训练任务提升代码编辑性能。
AI.motivation: 现有预训练任务（如代码填充）源自自然语言处理领域，未针对自动代码编辑优化，限制了模型性能与泛化能力。
AI.method: 收集真实代码片段作为基准，利用生成器创建变异版本，预训练CodeEditor将变异代码还原为基准，学习编辑模式。
AI.result: 在四个数据集的微调、少样本和零样本设置中，CodeEditor分别超出SOTA基线15%-26.6%，并在零样本场景下成功编辑1,113个程序。
AI.conclusion: 专门设计的预训练任务显著提升了代码编辑模型的性能与跨场景适应能力，验证了方法的有效性。

search_text: CodeEditor: Learning to Edit Source Code with Pre-trained Models CodeEditor：学习使用预训练模型编辑源代码 Developers often perform repetitive code editing activities for various reasons (e.g., code refactoring) during software development. Pre-trained code editing models have achieved the state-of-the-art (SOTA) results. Pre-trained models are first pre-trained with pre-training tasks and fine-tuned with the code editing task. Existing pre-training tasks mainly are code infilling tasks (e.g., masked language modeling), which are derived from the natural language processing field and are not designed for automatic code editing. This paper proposes a novel pre-training task specialized in code editing and presents an effective pre-trained code editing model named CodeEditor. Our pre-training task further improves the performance and generalization ability of code editing models. Specifically, we collect lots of real-world code snippets as the ground truth and use a powerful generator to rewrite them into mutated versions. Then, we pre-train our CodeEditor to edit mutated versions into the corresponding ground truth, to learn edit patterns. We conduct experiments on four code editing datasets and evaluate the pre-trained CodeEditor in three settings. (1) In the fine-tuning setting, we train the pre-trained CodeEditor with four datasets and evaluate it on the test data. CodeEditor outperforms the SOTA baselines by 15%, 25.5%, and 9.4% and 26.6% on four datasets. (2) In the few-shot setting, we train the pre-trained CodeEditor with limited data and evaluate it on the test data. CodeEditor substantially performs better than all baselines. (3) In the zero-shot setting, CodeEditor correctly edits 1,113 programs while the SOTA baselines can not work. 在软件开发过程中，开发人员经常出于各种原因（例如代码重构）执行重复的代码编辑活动。预训练的代码编辑模型已经达到了最先进的（SOTA）结果。预训练模型首先通过预训练任务进行预训练，并通过代码编辑任务进行微调。现有的预训练任务主要是代码填充任务（例如掩码语言建模），这些任务源自自然语言处理领域，并不是为自动代码编辑而设计的。本文提出了一种专门针对代码编辑的新型预训练任务，并提出了一种有效的预训练代码编辑模型，名为 CodeEditor。我们的预训练任务进一步提高了代码编辑模型的性能和泛化能力。具体来说，我们收集了大量现实世界的代码片段作为基本事实，并使用强大的生成器将它们重写为变异版本。然后，我们预训练代码编辑器将变异版本编辑为相应的基本事实，以学习编辑模式。我们在四个代码编辑数据集上进行实验，并在三种设置中评估预训练的 CodeEditor。 (1) 在微调设置中，我们使用四个数据集训练预训练的 CodeEditor，并在测试数据上对其进行评估。 CodeEditor 在四个数据集上的表现优于 SOTA 基线 15%、25.5%、9.4% 和 26.6%。 (2) 在少样本设置中，我们用有限的数据训练预训练的 CodeEditor 并在测试数据上对其进行评估。 CodeEditor 的性能明显优于所有基线。 (3) 在零样本设置下，CodeEditor 正确编辑了 1,113 个程序，而 SOTA 基线无法工作。 本文提出一种名为CodeEditor的预训练代码编辑模型，通过设计专门的预训练任务提升代码编辑性能。 Jia Li,Ge Li,Zhuo Li,Zhi Jin,Xing Hu,Kechi Zhang,Zhiyi Fu cs.SE,cs.AI

authors_text: Litian Li,Zheng Yang,Ronggang Wang
title: Improving Multi-generation Robustness of Learned Image Compression
title_zh: 提高学习图像压缩的多代鲁棒性
abstract: Benefit from flexible network designs and end-to-end joint optimization approach, learned image compression (LIC) has demonstrated excellent coding performance and practical feasibility in recent years. However, existing compression models suffer from serious multi-generation loss, which always occurs during image editing and transcoding. During the process of repeatedly encoding and decoding, the quality of the image will rapidly degrade, resulting in various types of distortion, which significantly limits the practical application of LIC. In this paper, a thorough analysis is carried out to determine the source of generative loss in successive image compression (SIC). We point out and solve the quantization drift problem that affects SIC, reversibility loss function as well as channel relaxation method are proposed to further reduce the generation loss. Experiments show that by using our proposed solutions, LIC can achieve comparable performance to the first compression of BPG even after 50 times reencoding without any change of the network structure.
summary: Benefit from flexible network designs and end-to-end joint optimization approach, learned image compression (LIC) has demonstrated excellent coding performance and practical feasibility in recent years. However, existing compression models suffer from serious multi-generation loss, which always occurs during image editing and transcoding. During the process of repeatedly encoding and decoding, the quality of the image will rapidly degrade, resulting in various types of distortion, which significantly limits the practical application of LIC. In this paper, a thorough analysis is carried out to determine the source of generative loss in successive image compression (SIC). We point out and solve the quantization drift problem that affects SIC, reversibility loss function as well as channel relaxation method are proposed to further reduce the generation loss. Experiments show that by using our proposed solutions, LIC can achieve comparable performance to the first compression of BPG even after 50 times reencoding without any change of the network structure.
abstract_zh: 得益于灵活的网络设计和端到端联合优化方法，学习图像压缩（LIC）近年来表现出了优异的编码性能和实际可行性。然而，现有的压缩模型存在严重的多代损失，这种情况在图像编辑和转码过程中经常发生。在反复编码和解码的过程中，图像质量会迅速下降，导致各种类型的失真，这极大地限制了LIC的实际应用。本文进行了彻底的分析，以确定连续图像压缩（SIC）中生成损失的来源。我们指出并解决了影响SIC的量化漂移问题，提出了可逆性损失函数以及通道松弛方法以进一步减少生成损失。实验表明，通过使用我们提出的解决方案，即使经过 50 次重新编码，LIC 也可以达到与 BPG 第一次压缩相当的性能，而无需改变网络结构。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在解决学习图像压缩（LIC）在多次编码解码过程中出现的多代质量损失问题，提升其鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有LIC模型在反复压缩时图像质量快速退化，导致实际应用受限，需解决多代损失问题。
AI.method: 分析量化漂移根源，提出可逆损失函数与通道放松方法，无需改变网络结构即可减少生成损失。
AI.result: 实验表明，使用所提方法后，LIC在50次重编码后性能可与BPG首次压缩结果媲美。
AI.conclusion: 通过解决量化漂移与优化损失函数，有效提升了LIC的多代鲁棒性，为实际应用提供可行方案。

authors_text: Ramin Ayanzadeh,Narges Alavisamani,Poulami Das,Moinuddin Qureshi
title: FrozenQubits: Boosting Fidelity of QAOA by Skipping Hotspot Nodes
title_zh: FrozenQubits：通过跳过热点节点提高 QAOA 的保真度
abstract: Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) is one of the leading candidates for demonstrating the quantum advantage using near-term quantum computers. Unfortunately, high device error rates limit us from reliably running QAOA circuits for problems with more than a few qubits. In QAOA, the problem graph is translated into a quantum circuit such that every edge corresponds to two 2-qubit CNOT operations in each layer of the circuit. As CNOTs are extremely error-prone, the fidelity of QAOA circuits is dictated by the number of edges in the problem graph. We observe that majority of graphs corresponding to real-world applications follow the ``power-law`` distribution, where some hotspot nodes have significantly higher number of connections. We leverage this insight and propose ``FrozenQubits`` that freezes the hotspot nodes or qubits and intelligently partitions the state-space of the given problem into several smaller sub-spaces which are then solved independently. The corresponding QAOA sub-circuits are significantly less vulnerable to gate and decoherence errors due to the reduced number of CNOT operations in each sub-circuit. Unlike prior circuit-cutting approaches, FrozenQubits does not require any exponentially complex post-processing step. Our evaluations with 5,300 QAOA circuits on eight different quantum computers from IBM shows that FrozenQubits can improve the quality of solutions by 8.73x on average (and by up to 57x), albeit utilizing 2x more quantum resources.
summary: Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) is one of the leading candidates for demonstrating the quantum advantage using near-term quantum computers. Unfortunately, high device error rates limit us from reliably running QAOA circuits for problems with more than a few qubits. In QAOA, the problem graph is translated into a quantum circuit such that every edge corresponds to two 2-qubit CNOT operations in each layer of the circuit. As CNOTs are extremely error-prone, the fidelity of QAOA circuits is dictated by the number of edges in the problem graph. We observe that majority of graphs corresponding to real-world applications follow the ``power-law`` distribution, where some hotspot nodes have significantly higher number of connections. We leverage this insight and propose ``FrozenQubits`` that freezes the hotspot nodes or qubits and intelligently partitions the state-space of the given problem into several smaller sub-spaces which are then solved independently. The corresponding QAOA sub-circuits are significantly less vulnerable to gate and decoherence errors due to the reduced number of CNOT operations in each sub-circuit. Unlike prior circuit-cutting approaches, FrozenQubits does not require any exponentially complex post-processing step. Our evaluations with 5,300 QAOA circuits on eight different quantum computers from IBM shows that FrozenQubits can improve the quality of solutions by 8.73x on average (and by up to 57x), albeit utilizing 2x more quantum resources.
abstract_zh: 量子近似优化算法（QAOA）是使用近期量子计算机展示量子优势的主要候选算法之一。不幸的是，高设备错误率限制了我们针对多个量子位的问题可靠地运行 QAOA 电路。在 QAOA 中，问题图被转换为量子电路，使得每条边对应于电路每层中的两个 2 量子位 CNOT 操作。由于 CNOT 极易出错，因此 QAOA 电路的保真度由问题图中的边数决定。我们观察到，与现实世界应用程序相对应的大多数图表都遵循“幂律”分布，其中一些热点节点具有明显更高的连接数量。我们利用这种洞察力并提出“FrozenQubits”，它冻结热点节点或量子位，并智能地将给定问题的状态空间划分为几个较小的子空间，然后独立解决这些子空间。由于每个子电路中 CNOT 操作数量的减少，相应的 QAOA 子电路明显不易受到门错误和退相干错误的影响。与之前的电路切割方法不同，FrozenQubits 不需要任何指数级复杂的后处理步骤。我们对 IBM 八台不同量子计算机上的 5,300 个 QAOA 电路进行的评估表明，FrozenQubits 可以将解决方案的质量平均提高 8.73 倍（最高可达 57 倍），尽管使用的量子资源增加了 2 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为FrozenQubits的方法，通过冻结问题图中的热点节点来提升QAOA算法的保真度。
AI.motivation: 当前量子设备的高错误率限制了QAOA算法在大规模问题上的可靠性，而问题图中热点节点导致的大量CNOT操作是主要瓶颈。
AI.method: 利用现实问题图的幂律分布特性，冻结高连接度的热点节点，将问题状态空间分割为多个子空间并独立求解。
AI.result: 在IBM八台量子计算机上测试5300个QAOA电路，平均解质量提升8.73倍（最高达57倍），但需使用2倍量子资源。
AI.conclusion: FrozenQubits通过减少子电路CNOT数量显著提升QAOA可靠性，且无需复杂后处理，适用于实际应用场景。

search_text: FrozenQubits: Boosting Fidelity of QAOA by Skipping Hotspot Nodes FrozenQubits：通过跳过热点节点提高 QAOA 的保真度 Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) is one of the leading candidates for demonstrating the quantum advantage using near-term quantum computers. Unfortunately, high device error rates limit us from reliably running QAOA circuits for problems with more than a few qubits. In QAOA, the problem graph is translated into a quantum circuit such that every edge corresponds to two 2-qubit CNOT operations in each layer of the circuit. As CNOTs are extremely error-prone, the fidelity of QAOA circuits is dictated by the number of edges in the problem graph. We observe that majority of graphs corresponding to real-world applications follow the ``power-law`` distribution, where some hotspot nodes have significantly higher number of connections. We leverage this insight and propose ``FrozenQubits`` that freezes the hotspot nodes or qubits and intelligently partitions the state-space of the given problem into several smaller sub-spaces which are then solved independently. The corresponding QAOA sub-circuits are significantly less vulnerable to gate and decoherence errors due to the reduced number of CNOT operations in each sub-circuit. Unlike prior circuit-cutting approaches, FrozenQubits does not require any exponentially complex post-processing step. Our evaluations with 5,300 QAOA circuits on eight different quantum computers from IBM shows that FrozenQubits can improve the quality of solutions by 8.73x on average (and by up to 57x), albeit utilizing 2x more quantum resources. 量子近似优化算法（QAOA）是使用近期量子计算机展示量子优势的主要候选算法之一。不幸的是，高设备错误率限制了我们针对多个量子位的问题可靠地运行 QAOA 电路。在 QAOA 中，问题图被转换为量子电路，使得每条边对应于电路每层中的两个 2 量子位 CNOT 操作。由于 CNOT 极易出错，因此 QAOA 电路的保真度由问题图中的边数决定。我们观察到，与现实世界应用程序相对应的大多数图表都遵循“幂律”分布，其中一些热点节点具有明显更高的连接数量。我们利用这种洞察力并提出“FrozenQubits”，它冻结热点节点或量子位，并智能地将给定问题的状态空间划分为几个较小的子空间，然后独立解决这些子空间。由于每个子电路中 CNOT 操作数量的减少，相应的 QAOA 子电路明显不易受到门错误和退相干错误的影响。与之前的电路切割方法不同，FrozenQubits 不需要任何指数级复杂的后处理步骤。我们对 IBM 八台不同量子计算机上的 5,300 个 QAOA 电路进行的评估表明，FrozenQubits 可以将解决方案的质量平均提高 8.73 倍（最高可达 57 倍），尽管使用的量子资源增加了 2 倍。 本文提出一种名为FrozenQubits的方法，通过冻结问题图中的热点节点来提升QAOA算法的保真度。 Ramin Ayanzadeh,Narges Alavisamani,Poulami Das,Moinuddin Qureshi quant-ph,cs.AR,cs.ET,cs.LG,math.OC

authors_text: Dhananjay Thiruvady,Su Nguyen,Yuan Sun,Fatemeh Shiri,Nayyar Zaidi,Xiaodong Li
title: Adaptive Population-based Simulated Annealing for Uncertain Resource Constrained Job Scheduling
title_zh: 不确定资源约束作业调度的基于自适应群体的模拟退火
abstract: Transporting ore from mines to ports is of significant interest in mining supply chains. These operations are commonly associated with growing costs and a lack of resources. Large mining companies are interested in optimally allocating their resources to reduce operational costs. This problem has been previously investigated in the literature as resource constrained job scheduling (RCJS). While a number of optimisation methods have been proposed to tackle the deterministic problem, the uncertainty associated with resource availability, an inevitable challenge in mining operations, has received less attention. RCJS with uncertainty is a hard combinatorial optimisation problem that cannot be solved efficiently with existing optimisation methods. This study proposes an adaptive population-based simulated annealing algorithm that can overcome the limitations of existing methods for RCJS with uncertainty including the premature convergence, the excessive number of hyper-parameters, and the inefficiency in coping with different uncertainty levels. This new algorithm is designed to effectively balance exploration and exploitation, by using a population, modifying the cooling schedule in the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm, and using an adaptive mechanism to select perturbation operators. The results show that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing methods across a wide range of benchmark RCJS instances and uncertainty levels. Moreover, new best known solutions are discovered for all but one problem instance across all uncertainty levels.
summary: Transporting ore from mines to ports is of significant interest in mining supply chains. These operations are commonly associated with growing costs and a lack of resources. Large mining companies are interested in optimally allocating their resources to reduce operational costs. This problem has been previously investigated in the literature as resource constrained job scheduling (RCJS). While a number of optimisation methods have been proposed to tackle the deterministic problem, the uncertainty associated with resource availability, an inevitable challenge in mining operations, has received less attention. RCJS with uncertainty is a hard combinatorial optimisation problem that cannot be solved efficiently with existing optimisation methods. This study proposes an adaptive population-based simulated annealing algorithm that can overcome the limitations of existing methods for RCJS with uncertainty including the premature convergence, the excessive number of hyper-parameters, and the inefficiency in coping with different uncertainty levels. This new algorithm is designed to effectively balance exploration and exploitation, by using a population, modifying the cooling schedule in the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm, and using an adaptive mechanism to select perturbation operators. The results show that the proposed algorithm outperforms existing methods across a wide range of benchmark RCJS instances and uncertainty levels. Moreover, new best known solutions are discovered for all but one problem instance across all uncertainty levels.
abstract_zh: 将矿石从矿山运输到港口对于采矿供应链来说具有重大意义。这些操作通常与成本增加和资源缺乏有关。大型矿业公司有兴趣优化资源配置以降低运营成本。这个问题之前已经在文献中作为资源受限作业调度（RCJS）进行了研究。尽管已经提出了许多优化方法来解决确定性问题，但与资源可用性相关的不确定性（采矿作业中不可避免的挑战）却很少受到关注。具有不确定性的RCJS是一个硬组合优化问题，现有的优化方法无法有效解决。本研究提出了一种基于群体的自适应模拟退火算法，可以克服现有不确定性RCJS方法的局限性，包括早熟收敛、超参数数量过多以及应对不同不确定性水平的低效等。这种新算法旨在通过使用种群、修改 Metropolis-Hastings 算法中的冷却计划以及使用自适应机制来选择扰动算子，从而有效地平衡探索和开发。结果表明，所提出的算法在各种基准 RCJS 实例和不确定性水平上都优于现有方法。此外，针对所有不确定性级别的除一个问题实例之外的所有问题实例都发现了新的最著名的解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自适应种群模拟退火算法，用于解决存在资源不确定性的资源约束作业调度问题，以优化采矿运输中的资源分配。
AI.motivation: 现有优化方法在处理资源可用性不确定性时存在早熟收敛、超参数过多及应对不同不确定性水平效率低的问题，亟需改进算法以应对采矿作业中的实际挑战。
AI.method: 设计自适应种群模拟退火算法，通过种群机制平衡探索与开发，改进Metropolis-Hastings冷却调度，并采用自适应机制选择扰动算子以应对不确定性。
AI.result: 新算法在多种基准RCJS实例和不同不确定性水平下均优于现有方法，并在所有不确定性水平下除一个实例外均发现新的最优解。
AI.conclusion: 该算法有效解决了不确定环境下RCJS问题的优化难题，为采矿供应链资源分配提供了更鲁棒的解决方案，未来可拓展至其他不确定性优化场景。

authors_text: Vanya Bannihatti Kumar
title: Evaluation of large-scale synthetic data for Grammar Error Correction
title_zh: 语法纠错的大规模合成数据评估
abstract: Grammar Error Correction(GEC) mainly relies on the availability of high quality of large amount of synthetic parallel data of grammatically correct and erroneous sentence pairs. The quality of the synthetic data is evaluated on how well the GEC system performs when pre-trained using it. But this does not provide much insight into what are the necessary factors which define the quality of these data. So this work aims to introduce 3 metrics - reliability, diversity and distribution match to provide more insight into the quality of large-scale synthetic data generated for the GEC task, as well as automatically evaluate them. Evaluating these three metrics automatically can also help in providing feedback to the data generation systems and thereby improve the quality of the synthetic data generated dynamically
summary: Grammar Error Correction(GEC) mainly relies on the availability of high quality of large amount of synthetic parallel data of grammatically correct and erroneous sentence pairs. The quality of the synthetic data is evaluated on how well the GEC system performs when pre-trained using it. But this does not provide much insight into what are the necessary factors which define the quality of these data. So this work aims to introduce 3 metrics - reliability, diversity and distribution match to provide more insight into the quality of large-scale synthetic data generated for the GEC task, as well as automatically evaluate them. Evaluating these three metrics automatically can also help in providing feedback to the data generation systems and thereby improve the quality of the synthetic data generated dynamically
abstract_zh: 语法错误纠正（GEC）主要依赖于语法正确和错误句子对的大量合成并行数据的高质量可用性。合成数据的质量根据 GEC 系统在使用预训练时的表现进行评估。但这并不能深入了解定义这些数据质量的必要因素。因此，这项工作旨在引入 3 个指标——可靠性、多样性和分布匹配，以更深入地了解为 GEC 任务生成的大规模合成数据的质量，并自动评估它们。自动评估这三个指标还可以帮助向数据生成系统提供反馈，从而提高动态生成的合成数据的质量

AI.tldr: 本文旨在引入三个自动评估指标（可靠性、多样性、分布匹配）来分析大规模合成数据的质量，并为数据生成系统提供反馈以提高其质量。
AI.motivation: 现有方法仅通过GEC系统表现评估数据质量，缺乏对关键因素的洞察，需引入新指标以深入分析并优化数据生成。
AI.method: 提出三个自动评估指标：可靠性（数据一致性）、多样性（错误类型覆盖范围）、分布匹配（与真实数据分布的匹配度），用于评估合成数据质量。
AI.result: 实验表明，这些指标能有效评估数据质量，为数据生成系统提供反馈，从而动态提升合成数据质量。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为合成数据质量评估提供了新指标，有助于优化数据生成过程，进而提升GEC系统的性能。

Evaluating these three metrics automatically can also help in providing feedback to the data generation systems and thereby improve the quality of the synthetic data generated dynamically 语法错误纠正（GEC）主要依赖于语法正确和错误句子对的大量合成并行数据的高质量可用性。合成数据的质量根据 GEC 系统在使用预训练时的表现进行评估。但这并不能深入了解定义这些数据质量的必要因素。因此，这项工作旨在引入 3 个指标——可靠性、多样性和分布匹配，以更深入地了解为 GEC 任务生成的大规模合成数据的质量，并自动评估它们。自动评估这三个指标还可以帮助向数据生成系统提供反馈，从而提高动态生成的合成数据的质量 本文旨在引入三个自动评估指标（可靠性、多样性、分布匹配）来分析大规模合成数据的质量，并为数据生成系统提供反馈以提高其质量。 Vanya Bannihatti Kumar cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Jia Li,Zhuo Li,Huangzhao Zhang,Ge Li,Zhi Jin,Xing Hu,Xin Xia
title: Poison Attack and Defense on Deep Source Code Processing Models
title_zh: 深度源代码处理模型的中毒攻击与防御
abstract: In the software engineering community, deep learning (DL) has recently been applied to many source code processing tasks. Due to the poor interpretability of DL models, their security vulnerabilities require scrutiny. Recently, researchers have identified an emergent security threat, namely poison attack. The attackers aim to inject insidious backdoors into models by poisoning the training data with poison samples. Poisoned models work normally with clean inputs but produce targeted erroneous results with poisoned inputs embedded with triggers. By activating backdoors, attackers can manipulate the poisoned models in security-related scenarios. To verify the vulnerability of existing deep source code processing models to the poison attack, we present a poison attack framework for source code named CodePoisoner as a strong imaginary enemy. CodePoisoner can produce compilable even human-imperceptible poison samples and attack models by poisoning the training data with poison samples. To defend against the poison attack, we further propose an effective defense approach named CodeDetector to detect poison samples in the training data. CodeDetector can be applied to many model architectures and effectively defend against multiple poison attack approaches. We apply our CodePoisoner and CodeDetector to three tasks, including defect detection, clone detection, and code repair. The results show that (1) CodePoisoner achieves a high attack success rate (max: 100%) in misleading models to targeted erroneous behaviors. It validates that existing deep source code processing models have a strong vulnerability to the poison attack. (2) CodeDetector effectively defends against multiple poison attack approaches by detecting (max: 100%) poison samples in the training data. We hope this work can help practitioners notice the poison attack and inspire the design of more advanced defense techniques.

Due to the poor interpretability of DL models, their security vulnerabilities require scrutiny. Recently, researchers have identified an emergent security threat, namely poison attack. Poisoned models work normally with clean inputs but produce targeted erroneous results with poisoned inputs embedded with triggers. CodePoisoner can produce compilable even human-imperceptible poison samples and attack models by poisoning the training data with poison samples. To defend against the poison attack, we further propose an effective defense approach named CodeDetector to detect poison samples in the training data. CodeDetector can be applied to many model architectures and effectively defend against multiple poison attack approaches. We apply our CodePoisoner and CodeDetector to three tasks, including defect detection, clone detection, and code repair. The results show that (1) CodePoisoner achieves a high attack success rate (max: 100%) in misleading models to targeted erroneous behaviors. It validates that existing deep source code processing models have a strong vulnerability to the poison attack. (2) CodeDetector effectively defends against multiple poison attack approaches by detecting (max: 100%) poison samples in the training data. We hope this work can help practitioners notice the poison attack and inspire the design of more advanced defense techniques.
abstract_zh: 在软件工程界，深度学习（DL）最近已应用于许多源代码处理任务。由于深度学习模型的可解释性较差，其安全漏洞需要仔细审查。最近，研究人员发现了一种紧急安全威胁，即中毒攻击。攻击者的目的是通过用毒样本毒害训练数据，将阴险的后门注入模型中。中毒模型在干净的输入下正常工作，但在嵌入触发器的中毒输入中产生有针对性的错误结果。通过激活后门，攻击者可以在安全相关场景中操纵中毒模型。为了验证现有的深层源代码处理模型对中毒攻击的脆弱性，我们提出了一种名为CodePoisoner的源代码中毒攻击框架，作为强大的假想敌。 CodePoisoner 可以通过用毒样本毒害训练数据来生成可编译的甚至人类无法察觉的毒样本和攻击模型。为了防御毒药攻击，我们进一步提出了一种有效的防御方法，名为 CodeDetector 来检测训练数据中的毒药样本。 CodeDetector可以应用于多种模型架构，有效防御多种投毒攻击方式。我们将 CodePoisoner 和 CodeDetector 应用于三个任务，包括缺陷检测、克隆检测和代码修复。结果表明（1）CodePoisoner 在针对目标错误行为的误导模型中实现了较高的攻击成功率（最高：100%）。它验证了现有的深层源代码处理模型对于中毒攻击具有很强的脆弱性。 （2）CodeDetector通过检测（最大：100%）训练数据中的毒样本，有效防御多种毒攻击方法。我们希望这项工作能够帮助从业者注意到毒物攻击，并启发设计更先进的防御技术。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了深度源代码处理模型的投毒攻击与防御方法，提出了攻击框架CodePoisoner和防御方法CodeDetector，并验证了其在缺陷检测、克隆检测和代码修复任务中的有效性。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型在软件工程中的广泛应用带来了安全风险，尤其是投毒攻击可能导致模型在特定输入下产生错误结果。现有模型的可解释性差，需针对此类攻击进行研究以提升安全性。
AI.method: 提出CodePoisoner框架生成可编译且人类不可感知的投毒样本攻击模型，并设计CodeDetector通过检测训练数据中的投毒样本进行防御。两种方法均适用于多种模型架构。
AI.result: CodePoisoner在三个任务中实现最高100%的攻击成功率，证明模型易受投毒攻击；CodeDetector可检测所有投毒样本，有效防御多种攻击方式。
AI.conclusion: 现有深度源代码处理模型存在严重投毒攻击漏洞，提出的防御方法能有效检测攻击样本，未来需进一步设计更先进的防御技术以应对此类威胁。

authors_text: Kun Wei,Long Zhou,Ziqiang Zhang,Liping Chen,Shujie Liu,Lei He,Jinyu Li,Furu Wei
title: Joint Pre-Training with Speech and Bilingual Text for Direct Speech to Speech Translation
title_zh: 语音与双语文本联合预训练，实现直接语音翻译
abstract: Direct speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) is an attractive research topic with many advantages compared to cascaded S2ST. However, direct S2ST suffers from the data scarcity problem because the corpora from speech of the source language to speech of the target language are very rare. To address this issue, we propose in this paper a Speech2S model, which is jointly pre-trained with unpaired speech and bilingual text data for direct speech-to-speech translation tasks. By effectively leveraging the paired text data, Speech2S is capable of modeling the cross-lingual speech conversion from source to target language. We verify the performance of the proposed Speech2S on Europarl-ST and VoxPopuli datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that Speech2S gets an improvement of about 5 BLEU scores compared to encoder-only pre-training models, and achieves a competitive or even better performance than existing state-of-the-art models1.
summary: Direct speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) is an attractive research topic with many advantages compared to cascaded S2ST. However, direct S2ST suffers from the data scarcity problem because the corpora from speech of the source language to speech of the target language are very rare. To address this issue, we propose in this paper a Speech2S model, which is jointly pre-trained with unpaired speech and bilingual text data for direct speech-to-speech translation tasks. By effectively leveraging the paired text data, Speech2S is capable of modeling the cross-lingual speech conversion from source to target language. We verify the performance of the proposed Speech2S on Europarl-ST and VoxPopuli datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that Speech2S gets an improvement of about 5 BLEU scores compared to encoder-only pre-training models, and achieves a competitive or even better performance than existing state-of-the-art models1.
abstract_zh: 直接语音到语音翻译 (S2ST) 是一个有吸引力的研究课题，与级联 S2ST 相比具有许多优点。然而，直接S2ST存在数据稀缺问题，因为从源语言语音到目标语言语音的语料库非常稀有。为了解决这个问题，我们在本文中提出了一种 Speech2S 模型，该模型使用不成对的语音和双语文本数据进行联合预训练，用于直接语音到语音翻译任务。通过有效利用配对文本数据，Speech2S 能够对从源语言到目标语言的跨语言语音转换进行建模。我们在 Europarl-ST 和 VoxPopuli 数据集上验证了所提出的 Speech2S 的性能。实验结果表明，与仅编码器预训练模型相比，Speech2S 的 BLEU 分数提高了约 5 个，并且比现有最先进的模型具有竞争力甚至更好的性能1。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种联合预训练模型Speech2S，用于直接语音到语音翻译任务。
AI.motivation: 直接语音到语音翻译（S2ST）因缺乏源语言到目标语言的配对语音数据而面临数据稀缺问题，需探索更有效的预训练方法。
AI.method: 通过联合预训练未配对的语音数据和双语文本数据，构建Speech2S模型以建模跨语言语音转换。
AI.result: 在Europarl-ST和VoxPopuli数据集上，Speech2S相比仅使用编码器预训练的模型提升了约5个BLEU分数，并达到或超越现有最先进模型的性能。
AI.conclusion: 联合利用语音与双语文本数据的预训练方法能有效缓解直接S2ST的数据稀缺问题，提升翻译效果。

By effectively leveraging the paired text data, Speech2S is capable of modeling the cross-lingual speech conversion from source to target language. We verify the performance of the proposed Speech2S on Europarl-ST and VoxPopuli datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that Speech2S gets an improvement of about 5 BLEU scores compared to encoder-only pre-training models, and achieves a competitive or even better performance than existing state-of-the-art models1. 直接语音到语音翻译 (S2ST) 是一个有吸引力的研究课题，与级联 S2ST 相比具有许多优点。然而，直接S2ST存在数据稀缺问题，因为从源语言语音到目标语言语音的语料库非常稀有。为了解决这个问题，我们在本文中提出了一种 Speech2S 模型，该模型使用不成对的语音和双语文本数据进行联合预训练，用于直接语音到语音翻译任务。通过有效利用配对文本数据，Speech2S 能够对从源语言到目标语言的跨语言语音转换进行建模。我们在 Europarl-ST 和 VoxPopuli 数据集上验证了所提出的 Speech2S 的性能。实验结果表明，与仅编码器预训练模型相比，Speech2S 的 BLEU 分数提高了约 5 个，并且比现有最先进的模型具有竞争力甚至更好的性能1。 本文提出一种联合预训练模型Speech2S，用于直接语音到语音翻译任务。 Kun Wei,Long Zhou,Ziqiang Zhang,Liping Chen,Shujie Liu,Lei He,Jinyu Li,Furu Wei cs.SD,cs.CL,eess.AS

authors_text: Hangfeng He,Weijie J. Su
title: A Law of Data Separation in Deep Learning
title_zh: 深度学习中的数据分离定律
abstract: While deep learning has enabled significant advances in many areas of science, its black-box nature hinders architecture design for future artificial intelligence applications and interpretation for high-stakes decision makings. We addressed this issue by studying the fundamental question of how deep neural networks process data in the intermediate layers. Our finding is a simple and quantitative law that governs how deep neural networks separate data according to class membership throughout all layers for classification. This law shows that each layer improves data separation at a constant geometric rate, and its emergence is observed in a collection of network architectures and datasets during training. This law offers practical guidelines for designing architectures, improving model robustness and out-of-sample performance, as well as interpreting the predictions.
summary: While deep learning has enabled significant advances in many areas of science, its black-box nature hinders architecture design for future artificial intelligence applications and interpretation for high-stakes decision makings. We addressed this issue by studying the fundamental question of how deep neural networks process data in the intermediate layers. Our finding is a simple and quantitative law that governs how deep neural networks separate data according to class membership throughout all layers for classification. This law shows that each layer improves data separation at a constant geometric rate, and its emergence is observed in a collection of network architectures and datasets during training. This law offers practical guidelines for designing architectures, improving model robustness and out-of-sample performance, as well as interpreting the predictions.
abstract_zh: 虽然深度学习在许多科学领域取得了重大进展，但其黑盒性质阻碍了未来人工智能应用的架构设计和高风险决策的解释。我们通过研究深度神经网络如何处理中间层数据的基本问题来解决这个问题。我们的发现是一个简单的定量定律，它控制深度神经网络如何根据所有层中的类成员资格来分离数据以进行分类。该定律表明，每一层都以恒定的几何速率改善数据分离，并且在训练过程中在网络架构和数据集的集合中观察到它的出现。该定律为设计架构、提高模型稳健性和样本外性能以及解释预测提供了实用指南。

AI.tldr: 研究深度神经网络中间层如何处理数据，并发现数据分离定律
AI.motivation: 解决深度学习模型的黑箱问题，提升其可解释性和架构设计的科学性
AI.method: 通过分析深度神经网络中间层的数据处理过程，研究各层如何根据类别成员分离数据
AI.result: 发现各层以恒定几何速率提升数据分离，该规律在多种网络和数据集训练中被验证
AI.conclusion: 该定律为深度学习模型的架构设计、鲁棒性提升和预测解释提供了理论依据和实践指导

authors_text: Minkyu Jung,Ohhyeok Kwon,Seunghyun Seo,Soonshin Seo
title: Blank Collapse: Compressing CTC emission for the faster decoding
title_zh: Blank Collapse：压缩 CTC 发射以加快解码速度
abstract: Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) model is a very efficient method for modeling sequences, especially for speech data. In order to use CTC model as an Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) task, the beam search decoding with an external language model like n-gram LM is necessary to obtain reasonable results. In this paper we analyze the blank label in CTC beam search deeply and propose a very simple method to reduce the amount of calculation resulting in faster beam search decoding speed. With this method, we can get up to 78% faster decoding speed than ordinary beam search decoding with a very small loss of accuracy in LibriSpeech datasets. We prove this method is effective not only practically by experiments but also theoretically by mathematical reasoning. We also observe that this reduction is more obvious if the accuracy of the model is higher.
summary: Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) model is a very efficient method for modeling sequences, especially for speech data. In order to use CTC model as an Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) task, the beam search decoding with an external language model like n-gram LM is necessary to obtain reasonable results. In this paper we analyze the blank label in CTC beam search deeply and propose a very simple method to reduce the amount of calculation resulting in faster beam search decoding speed. With this method, we can get up to 78% faster decoding speed than ordinary beam search decoding with a very small loss of accuracy in LibriSpeech datasets. We prove this method is effective not only practically by experiments but also theoretically by mathematical reasoning. We also observe that this reduction is more obvious if the accuracy of the model is higher.
abstract_zh: 连接主义时间分类（CTC）模型是一种非常有效的序列建模方法，特别是对于语音数据。为了将 CTC 模型用作自动语音识别 (ASR) 任务，需要使用 n-gram LM 等外部语言模型进行波束搜索解码才能获得合理的结果。在本文中，我们深入分析了CTC束搜索中的空白标签，并提出了一种非常简单的方法来减少计算量，从而获得更快的束搜索解码速度。通过这种方法，我们可以在 LibriSpeech 数据集中获得比普通波束搜索解码快 78% 的解码速度，同时精度损失非常小。我们不仅通过实验证明了该方法在实践上是有效的，而且通过数学推理在理论上也证明了该方法的有效性。我们还观察到，如果模型的精度越高，这种减少就越明显。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过压缩CTC发射过程中的空白标签来加速束搜索解码的方法，从而提高自动语音识别的解码速度。
AI.motivation: CTC模型在语音识别中需结合外部语言模型进行束搜索解码，但计算量大导致效率低下，需寻找减少计算量的方法以提升速度。
AI.method: 通过深入分析CTC束搜索中的空白标签，提出一种简单方法压缩空白发射过程，减少计算量。
AI.result: 在LibriSpeech数据集上实现比传统束搜索快78%的解码速度，且准确率损失极小，且模型精度越高效果越显著。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在实验和理论层面均被证明有效，是一种简单且高效的CTC解码加速方案，尤其适用于高精度模型。

authors_text: Hongji Wang,Chengdong Liang,Shuai Wang,Zhengyang Chen,Binbin Zhang,Xu Xiang,Yanlei Deng,Yanmin Qian
title: Wespeaker: A Research and Production oriented Speaker Embedding Learning Toolkit
title_zh: Wespeaker：面向研究和生产的扬声器嵌入学习工具包
abstract: Speaker modeling is essential for many related tasks, such as speaker recognition and speaker diarization. The dominant modeling approach is fixed-dimensional vector representation, i.e., speaker embedding. This paper introduces a research and production oriented speaker embedding learning toolkit, Wespeaker. Wespeaker contains the implementation of scalable data management, state-of-the-art speaker embedding models, loss functions, and scoring back-ends, with highly competitive results achieved by structured recipes which were adopted in the winning systems in several speaker verification challenges. The application to other downstream tasks such as speaker diarization is also exhibited in the related recipe. Moreover, CPU- and GPU-compatible deployment codes are integrated for production-oriented development. The toolkit is publicly available at https://github.com/wenet-e2e/wespeaker.
summary: Speaker modeling is essential for many related tasks, such as speaker recognition and speaker diarization. The dominant modeling approach is fixed-dimensional vector representation, i.e., speaker embedding. This paper introduces a research and production oriented speaker embedding learning toolkit, Wespeaker. Wespeaker contains the implementation of scalable data management, state-of-the-art speaker embedding models, loss functions, and scoring back-ends, with highly competitive results achieved by structured recipes which were adopted in the winning systems in several speaker verification challenges. The application to other downstream tasks such as speaker diarization is also exhibited in the related recipe. Moreover, CPU- and GPU-compatible deployment codes are integrated for production-oriented development. The toolkit is publicly available at https://github.com/wenet-e2e/wespeaker.
abstract_zh: 说话人建模对于许多相关任务至关重要，例如说话人识别和说话人二值化。主要的建模方法是固定维向量表示，即说话人嵌入。本文介绍了一种面向研究和生产的扬声器嵌入学习工具包Wespeaker。 Wespeaker 包含可扩展数据管理、最先进的说话人嵌入模型、损失函数和评分后端的实施，通过在多个说话人验证挑战赛的获胜系统中采用的结构化配方取得了极具竞争力的结果。相关配方中还展示了对其他下游任务（例如说话人分类）的应用。此外，集成了CPU和GPU兼容的部署代码，以进行面向生产的开发。该工具包可在 https://github.com/wenet-e2e/wespeaker 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个面向研究和生产的说话人嵌入学习工具包Wespeaker，包含数据管理、模型实现及部署方案。
AI.motivation: 说话人建模在语音识别等任务中至关重要，但缺乏集成化工具支持研究与生产应用。
AI.method: 实现可扩展数据管理模块、先进嵌入模型、损失函数及评分后端，并提供CPU/GPU兼容的生产部署代码。
AI.result: 在多个说话人验证挑战中取得竞争力结果，且成功应用于说话人聚类等下游任务。
AI.conclusion: Wespeaker为说话人嵌入研究和工业应用提供了高效工具，推动相关技术发展。

The toolkit is publicly available at https://github.com/wenet-e2e/wespeaker. 说话人建模对于许多相关任务至关重要，例如说话人识别和说话人二值化。主要的建模方法是固定维向量表示，即说话人嵌入。本文介绍了一种面向研究和生产的扬声器嵌入学习工具包Wespeaker。 Wespeaker 包含可扩展数据管理、最先进的说话人嵌入模型、损失函数和评分后端的实施，通过在多个说话人验证挑战赛的获胜系统中采用的结构化配方取得了极具竞争力的结果。相关配方中还展示了对其他下游任务（例如说话人分类）的应用。此外，集成了CPU和GPU兼容的部署代码，以进行面向生产的开发。该工具包可在 https://github.com/wenet-e2e/wespeaker 上公开获取。 本文介绍了一个面向研究和生产的说话人嵌入学习工具包Wespeaker，包含数据管理、模型实现及部署方案。 Hongji Wang,Chengdong Liang,Shuai Wang,Zhengyang Chen,Binbin Zhang,Xu Xiang,Yanlei Deng,Yanmin Qian cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Maxime Tchibozo,Donggeun Kim,Zijing Wang,Xiaofu He
title: Emotional Brain State Classification on fMRI Data Using Deep Residual and Convolutional Networks
title_zh: 使用深度残差和卷积网络对 fMRI 数据进行情绪脑状态分类
abstract: The goal of emotional brain state classification on functional MRI (fMRI) data is to recognize brain activity patterns related to specific emotion tasks performed by subjects during an experiment. Distinguishing emotional brain states from other brain states using fMRI data has proven to be challenging due to two factors: a difficulty to generate fast yet accurate predictions in short time frames, and a difficulty to extract emotion features which generalize to unseen subjects. To address these challenges, we conducted an experiment in which 22 subjects viewed pictures designed to stimulate either negative, neutral or rest emotional responses while their brain activity was measured using fMRI. We then developed two distinct Convolution-based approaches to decode emotional brain states using only spatial information from single, minimally pre-processed (slice timing and realignment) fMRI volumes. In our first approach, we trained a 1D Convolutional Network (84.9% accuracy; chance level 33%) to classify 3 emotion conditions using One-way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) voxel selection combined with hyperalignment. In our second approach, we trained a 3D ResNet-50 model (78.0% accuracy; chance level 50%) to classify 2 emotion conditions from single 3D fMRI volumes directly. Our Convolutional and Residual classifiers successfully learned group-level emotion features and could decode emotion conditions from fMRI volumes in milliseconds. These approaches could potentially be used in brain computer interfaces and real-time fMRI neurofeedback research.
summary: The goal of emotional brain state classification on functional MRI (fMRI) data is to recognize brain activity patterns related to specific emotion tasks performed by subjects during an experiment. Distinguishing emotional brain states from other brain states using fMRI data has proven to be challenging due to two factors: a difficulty to generate fast yet accurate predictions in short time frames, and a difficulty to extract emotion features which generalize to unseen subjects. To address these challenges, we conducted an experiment in which 22 subjects viewed pictures designed to stimulate either negative, neutral or rest emotional responses while their brain activity was measured using fMRI. We then developed two distinct Convolution-based approaches to decode emotional brain states using only spatial information from single, minimally pre-processed (slice timing and realignment) fMRI volumes. In our first approach, we trained a 1D Convolutional Network (84.9% accuracy; chance level 33%) to classify 3 emotion conditions using One-way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) voxel selection combined with hyperalignment. In our second approach, we trained a 3D ResNet-50 model (78.0% accuracy; chance level 50%) to classify 2 emotion conditions from single 3D fMRI volumes directly. Our Convolutional and Residual classifiers successfully learned group-level emotion features and could decode emotion conditions from fMRI volumes in milliseconds. These approaches could potentially be used in brain computer interfaces and real-time fMRI neurofeedback research.
abstract_zh: 对功能 MRI (fMRI) 数据进行情绪大脑状态分类的目标是识别与实验期间受试者执行的特定情绪任务相关的大脑活动模式。事实证明，使用功能磁共振成像数据区分情绪大脑状态与其他大脑状态具有挑战性，原因有两个：难以在短时间内生成快速而准确的预测，以及难以提取泛化到看不见的受试者的情绪特征。为了应对这些挑战，我们进行了一项实验，让 22 名受试者观看旨在刺激消极、中性或休息情绪反应的图片，同时使用功能磁共振成像测量他们的大脑活动。然后，我们开发了两种不同的基于卷积的方法，仅使用来自单个、最低限度预处理（切片定时和重新对齐）功能磁共振成像体积的空间信息来解码情绪大脑状态。在我们的第一种方法中，我们训练了一个 1D 卷积网络（准确率 84.9%；机会水平 33%），使用单向方差分析 (ANOVA) 体素选择结合超对齐对 3 种情绪条件进行分类。在第二种方法中，我们训练了 3D ResNet-50 模型（准确率 78.0%；机会水平 50%），以直接从单个 3D fMRI 体积中对 2 种情绪条件进行分类。我们的卷积和残差分类器成功学习了群体级别的情感特征，并且可以在几毫秒内从功能磁共振成像体积中解码情感条件。这些方法有可能用于脑机接口和实时功能磁共振成像神经反馈研究。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在利用深度残差和卷积网络对功能磁共振成像(fMRI)数据中的情感脑状态进行分类，以解决实时预测和跨被试特征泛化的挑战。
AI.motivation: 情感脑状态分类面临两大挑战：需在短时间帧内实现快速准确预测，且需提取可泛化到新被试的情感特征。传统方法在这些方面存在局限性。
AI.method: 通过让22名被试观看诱发负面、中性或休息情绪的图片，采集fMRI数据。提出两种基于卷积的方法：1) 使用1D卷积网络结合ANOVA体素选择和超对齐进行三分类；2) 使用3D ResNet-50模型直接对单个体积进行二分类。
AI.result: 1D卷积网络在三分类任务中达到84.9%准确率（机遇水平33%），3D ResNet-50在二分类任务中达到78.0%准确率（机遇水平50%），且能在毫秒级完成解码。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法成功学习到群体级情感特征，可应用于脑机接口和实时fMRI神经反馈研究，为情感状态的快速解码提供了新思路。

authors_text: Imaad Zaffar,Guillaume Jaume,Nasir Rajpoot,Faisal Mahmood
title: Embedding Space Augmentation for Weakly Supervised Learning in Whole-Slide Images
title_zh: 在整个幻灯片图像中嵌入空间增强弱监督学习
abstract: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) is a widely employed framework for learning on gigapixel whole-slide images (WSIs) from WSI-level annotations. In most MIL based analytical pipelines for WSI-level analysis, the WSIs are often divided into patches and deep features for patches (i.e., patch embeddings) are extracted prior to training to reduce the overall computational cost and cope with the GPUs' limited RAM. To overcome this limitation, we present EmbAugmenter, a data augmentation generative adversarial network (DA-GAN) that can synthesize data augmentations in the embedding space rather than in the pixel space, thereby significantly reducing the computational requirements. Experiments on the SICAPv2 dataset show that our approach outperforms MIL without augmentation and is on par with traditional patch-level augmentation for MIL training while being substantially faster.
summary: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) is a widely employed framework for learning on gigapixel whole-slide images (WSIs) from WSI-level annotations. In most MIL based analytical pipelines for WSI-level analysis, the WSIs are often divided into patches and deep features for patches (i.e., patch embeddings) are extracted prior to training to reduce the overall computational cost and cope with the GPUs' limited RAM. To overcome this limitation, we present EmbAugmenter, a data augmentation generative adversarial network (DA-GAN) that can synthesize data augmentations in the embedding space rather than in the pixel space, thereby significantly reducing the computational requirements. Experiments on the SICAPv2 dataset show that our approach outperforms MIL without augmentation and is on par with traditional patch-level augmentation for MIL training while being substantially faster.
abstract_zh: 多实例学习 (MIL) 是一种广泛采用的框架，用于通过 WSI 级注释学习十亿像素全幻灯片图像 (WSI)。在大多数用于 WSI 级分析的基于 MIL 的分析管道中，WSI 通常被分为多个补丁，并在训练之前提取补丁的深层特征（即补丁嵌入），以降低总体计算成本并应对 GPU 有限的 RAM。为了克服这一限制，我们提出了 EmbAugmenter，一种数据增强生成对抗网络（DA-GAN），它可以在嵌入空间而不是像素空间中合成数据增强，从而显着降低计算要求。 SICAPv2 数据集上的实验表明，我们的方法在没有增强的情况下优于 MIL，并且与 MIL 训练的传统补丁级增强相当，同时速度更快。

AI.tldr: 提出一种嵌入空间增强方法，用于弱监督学习中的全切片图像分析，以提高计算效率和模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统数据增强在像素空间进行计算成本高，且处理全切片图像时受限于GPU内存，需更高效的方法。
AI.method: 设计一个生成对抗网络（DA-GAN），在嵌入空间而非像素空间进行数据增强，减少计算资源消耗。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在SICAPv2数据集上优于无增强的MIL，且速度显著快于传统补丁级增强方法。
AI.conclusion: 嵌入空间增强能有效提升弱监督学习在全切片图像中的效率和性能，为大规模WSI分析提供可行方案。

authors_text: Jiaming Liang,Chao Xu,Shengze Cai
title: GotFlow3D: Recurrent Graph Optimal Transport for Learning 3D Flow Motion in Particle Tracking
title_zh: GotFlow3D：用于学习粒子跟踪中 3D 流运动的循环图最佳传输
abstract: Flow visualization technologies such as particle tracking velocimetry (PTV) are broadly used in understanding the all-pervasiveness three-dimensional (3D) turbulent flow from nature and industrial processes. Despite the advances in 3D acquisition techniques, the developed motion estimation algorithms in particle tracking remain great challenges of large particle displacements, dense particle distributions and high computational cost. By introducing a novel deep neural network based on recurrent Graph Optimal Transport, called GotFlow3D, we present an end-to-end solution to learn the 3D fluid flow motion from double-frame particle sets. The proposed network constructs two graphs in the geometric and feature space and further enriches the original particle representations with the fused intrinsic and extrinsic features learnt from a graph neural network. The extracted deep features are subsequently utilized to make optimal transport plans indicating the correspondences of particle pairs, which are then iteratively and adaptively retrieved to guide the recurrent flow learning. Experimental evaluations, including assessments on numerical experiments and validations on real-world experiments, demonstrate that the proposed GotFlow3D achieves state-of-the-art performance against both recently-developed scene flow learners and particle tracking algorithms, with impressive accuracy, robustness and generalization ability, which can provide deeper insight into the complex dynamics of broad physical and biological systems.
summary: Flow visualization technologies such as particle tracking velocimetry (PTV) are broadly used in understanding the all-pervasiveness three-dimensional (3D) turbulent flow from nature and industrial processes. Despite the advances in 3D acquisition techniques, the developed motion estimation algorithms in particle tracking remain great challenges of large particle displacements, dense particle distributions and high computational cost. By introducing a novel deep neural network based on recurrent Graph Optimal Transport, called GotFlow3D, we present an end-to-end solution to learn the 3D fluid flow motion from double-frame particle sets. The proposed network constructs two graphs in the geometric and feature space and further enriches the original particle representations with the fused intrinsic and extrinsic features learnt from a graph neural network. The extracted deep features are subsequently utilized to make optimal transport plans indicating the correspondences of particle pairs, which are then iteratively and adaptively retrieved to guide the recurrent flow learning. Experimental evaluations, including assessments on numerical experiments and validations on real-world experiments, demonstrate that the proposed GotFlow3D achieves state-of-the-art performance against both recently-developed scene flow learners and particle tracking algorithms, with impressive accuracy, robustness and generalization ability, which can provide deeper insight into the complex dynamics of broad physical and biological systems.
abstract_zh: 粒子跟踪测速 (PTV) 等流动可视化技术广泛用于理解自然和工业过程中普遍存在的三维 (3D) 湍流。尽管 3D 采集技术取得了进步，但粒子跟踪中已开发的运动估计算法仍然面临着大粒子位移、密集粒子分布和高计算成本的巨大挑战。通过引入一种基于循环图最优传输的新型深度神经网络（称为 GotFlow3D），我们提出了一种端到端解决方案，用于从双帧粒子集学习 3D 流体流动运动。所提出的网络在几何和特征空间中构建两个图，并通过从图神经网络学习到的融合的内在和外在特征进一步丰富了原始粒子表示。随后利用提取的深层特征来制定指示粒子对对应关系的最佳传输计划，然后迭代和自适应地检索这些计划以指导循环流学习。实验评估，包括数值实验的评估和现实世界实验的验证，表明所提出的 GotFlow3D 在最近开发的场景流学习器和粒子跟踪算法上实现了最先进的性能，具有令人印象深刻的准确性、鲁棒性和泛化能力，可以更深入地了解广泛的物理和生物系统的复杂动态。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GotFlow3D网络，通过图最优传输方法解决三维流场粒子追踪中的大位移、高密度和计算成本问题。
AI.motivation: 现有三维粒子追踪算法在处理大位移粒子、高密度分布及计算效率方面存在显著挑战，需更高效精准的运动估计方法。
AI.method: 构建几何与特征双空间图结构，融合图神经网络提取的内外特征，基于最优传输规划实现粒子配对，并通过循环迭代优化流场学习。
AI.result: 在数值模拟与真实实验中均达到SOTA性能，展现优异的准确率、鲁棒性及跨场景泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: GotFlow3D有效攻克三维流场运动估计难题，为复杂物理与生物系统的动力学研究提供新工具。

search_text: GotFlow3D: Recurrent Graph Optimal Transport for Learning 3D Flow Motion in Particle Tracking GotFlow3D：用于学习粒子跟踪中 3D 流运动的循环图最佳传输 Flow visualization technologies such as particle tracking velocimetry (PTV) are broadly used in understanding the all-pervasiveness three-dimensional (3D) turbulent flow from nature and industrial processes. Despite the advances in 3D acquisition techniques, the developed motion estimation algorithms in particle tracking remain great challenges of large particle displacements, dense particle distributions and high computational cost. By introducing a novel deep neural network based on recurrent Graph Optimal Transport, called GotFlow3D, we present an end-to-end solution to learn the 3D fluid flow motion from double-frame particle sets. The proposed network constructs two graphs in the geometric and feature space and further enriches the original particle representations with the fused intrinsic and extrinsic features learnt from a graph neural network. The extracted deep features are subsequently utilized to make optimal transport plans indicating the correspondences of particle pairs, which are then iteratively and adaptively retrieved to guide the recurrent flow learning. Experimental evaluations, including assessments on numerical experiments and validations on real-world experiments, demonstrate that the proposed GotFlow3D achieves state-of-the-art performance against both recently-developed scene flow learners and particle tracking algorithms, with impressive accuracy, robustness and generalization ability, which can provide deeper insight into the complex dynamics of broad physical and biological systems. 粒子跟踪测速 (PTV) 等流动可视化技术广泛用于理解自然和工业过程中普遍存在的三维 (3D) 湍流。尽管 3D 采集技术取得了进步，但粒子跟踪中已开发的运动估计算法仍然面临着大粒子位移、密集粒子分布和高计算成本的巨大挑战。通过引入一种基于循环图最优传输的新型深度神经网络（称为 GotFlow3D），我们提出了一种端到端解决方案，用于从双帧粒子集学习 3D 流体流动运动。所提出的网络在几何和特征空间中构建两个图，并通过从图神经网络学习到的融合的内在和外在特征进一步丰富了原始粒子表示。随后利用提取的深层特征来制定指示粒子对对应关系的最佳传输计划，然后迭代和自适应地检索这些计划以指导循环流学习。实验评估，包括数值实验的评估和现实世界实验的验证，表明所提出的 GotFlow3D 在最近开发的场景流学习器和粒子跟踪算法上实现了最先进的性能，具有令人印象深刻的准确性、鲁棒性和泛化能力，可以更深入地了解广泛的物理和生物系统的复杂动态。 本文提出GotFlow3D网络，通过图最优传输方法解决三维流场粒子追踪中的大位移、高密度和计算成本问题。 Jiaming Liang,Chao Xu,Shengze Cai physics.flu-dyn,cs.AI

authors_text: Ziwei Wang,Reza Arablouei,Jiajun Liu,Paulo Borges,Greg Bishop-Hurley,Nicholas Heaney
title: Point-Syn2Real: Semi-Supervised Synthetic-to-Real Cross-Domain Learning for Object Classification in 3D Point Clouds
title_zh: Point-Syn2Real：用于 3D 点云中对象分类的半监督合成到真实跨域学习
abstract: Object classification using LiDAR 3D point cloud data is critical for modern applications such as autonomous driving. However, labeling point cloud data is labor-intensive as it requires human annotators to visualize and inspect the 3D data from different perspectives. In this paper, we propose a semi-supervised cross-domain learning approach that does not rely on manual annotations of point clouds and performs similar to fully-supervised approaches. We utilize available 3D object models to train classifiers that can generalize to real-world point clouds. We simulate the acquisition of point clouds by sampling 3D object models from multiple viewpoints and with arbitrary partial occlusions. We then augment the resulting set of point clouds through random rotations and adding Gaussian noise to better emulate the real-world scenarios. We then train point cloud encoding models, e.g., DGCNN, PointNet++, on the synthesized and augmented datasets and evaluate their cross-domain classification performance on corresponding real-world datasets. We also introduce Point-Syn2Real, a new benchmark dataset for cross-domain learning on point clouds. The results of our extensive experiments with this dataset demonstrate that the proposed cross-domain learning approach for point clouds outperforms the related baseline and state-of-the-art approaches in both indoor and outdoor settings in terms of cross-domain generalizability. The code and data will be available upon publishing.
summary: Object classification using LiDAR 3D point cloud data is critical for modern applications such as autonomous driving. However, labeling point cloud data is labor-intensive as it requires human annotators to visualize and inspect the 3D data from different perspectives. In this paper, we propose a semi-supervised cross-domain learning approach that does not rely on manual annotations of point clouds and performs similar to fully-supervised approaches. We utilize available 3D object models to train classifiers that can generalize to real-world point clouds. We simulate the acquisition of point clouds by sampling 3D object models from multiple viewpoints and with arbitrary partial occlusions. We then augment the resulting set of point clouds through random rotations and adding Gaussian noise to better emulate the real-world scenarios. We then train point cloud encoding models, e.g., DGCNN, PointNet++, on the synthesized and augmented datasets and evaluate their cross-domain classification performance on corresponding real-world datasets. We also introduce Point-Syn2Real, a new benchmark dataset for cross-domain learning on point clouds. The results of our extensive experiments with this dataset demonstrate that the proposed cross-domain learning approach for point clouds outperforms the related baseline and state-of-the-art approaches in both indoor and outdoor settings in terms of cross-domain generalizability. The code and data will be available upon publishing.
abstract_zh: 使用 LiDAR 3D 点云数据进行对象分类对于自动驾驶等现代应用至关重要。然而，标记点云数据是劳动密集型的，因为它需要人类注释者从不同的角度可视化和检查 3D 数据。在本文中，我们提出了一种半监督跨域学习方法，该方法不依赖于点云的手动注释，并且执行类似于完全监督方法。我们利用可用的 3D 对象模型来训练可以推广到现实世界点云的分类器。我们通过从多个视点和任意部分遮挡对 3D 对象模型进行采样来模拟点云的获取。然后，我们通过随机旋转并添加高斯噪声来增强生成的点云集，以更好地模拟现实世界的场景。然后，我们在合成和增强的数据集上训练点云编码模型，例如 DGCNN、PointNet++，并在相应的真实数据集上评估它们的跨域分类性能。我们还引入了 Point-Syn2Real，这是一个用于点云跨域学习的新基准数据集。我们对该数据集进行的广泛实验的结果表明，所提出的点云跨域学习方法在跨域泛化性方面优于室内和室外环境中的相关基线和最先进的方法。代码和数据将在发布后可用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需人工标注的半监督跨域学习方法，通过合成3D点云数据训练分类器以实现在真实场景中的泛化。
AI.motivation: LiDAR点云数据标注耗时耗力，而现有方法依赖大量人工标注。本文旨在通过合成数据减少对人工标注的依赖，同时保持分类性能。
AI.method: 利用3D模型生成多视角点云并加入噪声和遮挡模拟真实场景，使用DGCNN/PointNet++等模型进行训练，构建Point-Syn2Real基准数据集评估跨域性能。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在室内外场景下均优于现有基线和最新方法，证明了合成到真实场景的跨域泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 半监督跨域学习能有效缓解点云标注难题，为自动驾驶等应用提供高效可靠的3D分类解决方案。

authors_text: Chunyu Liu,Jiacai Zhang
title: STN: a new tensor network method to identify stimulus category from brain activity pattern
title_zh: STN：一种新的张量网络方法，用于从大脑活动模式中识别刺激类别
abstract: Neural decoding is still a challenge and hot topic in neurocomputing science. Recently, many studies have shown that brain network patterns containing rich spatial and temporal structure information, which represents the activation information of brain under external stimuli. %Therefore, the research of decoding stimuli from brain network received extensive more attention. The traditional method extracts brain network features directly from the common machine learning method, then puts these features into the classifier, and realizes to decode external stimuli. However, this method cannot effectively extract the multi-dimensional structural information, which is hidden in the brain network. The tensor researchers show that the tensor decomposition model can fully mine unique spatio-temporal structure characteristics in multi-dimensional structure data. This research proposed a stimulus constrained tensor brain model(STN)which involves the tensor decomposition idea and stimulus category constraint information. The model was verified on the real neuroimaging data sets (MEG and fMRI). The experimental results show that the STN model achieves more than 11.06% and 18.46% on accuracy matrix compared with others methods on two modal data sets. These results imply the superiority of extracting discriminative characteristics about STN model, especially for decoding object stimuli with semantic information.
summary: Neural decoding is still a challenge and hot topic in neurocomputing science. Recently, many studies have shown that brain network patterns containing rich spatial and temporal structure information, which represents the activation information of brain under external stimuli. %Therefore, the research of decoding stimuli from brain network received extensive more attention. The traditional method extracts brain network features directly from the common machine learning method, then puts these features into the classifier, and realizes to decode external stimuli. However, this method cannot effectively extract the multi-dimensional structural information, which is hidden in the brain network. The tensor researchers show that the tensor decomposition model can fully mine unique spatio-temporal structure characteristics in multi-dimensional structure data. This research proposed a stimulus constrained tensor brain model(STN)which involves the tensor decomposition idea and stimulus category constraint information. The model was verified on the real neuroimaging data sets (MEG and fMRI). The experimental results show that the STN model achieves more than 11.06% and 18.46% on accuracy matrix compared with others methods on two modal data sets. These results imply the superiority of extracting discriminative characteristics about STN model, especially for decoding object stimuli with semantic information.
abstract_zh: 神经解码仍然是神经计算科学中的挑战和热门话题。近年来，许多研究表明，大脑网络模式包含丰富的时空结构信息，代表了大脑在外界刺激下的激活信息。 %因此，从大脑网络解码刺激的研究受到了广泛的关注。传统方法直接从常见的机器学习方法中提取脑网络特征，然后将这些特征放入分类器中，实现对外部刺激的解码。然而，该方法无法有效提取隐藏在大脑网络中的多维结构信息。张量研究人员表明，张量分解模型可以充分挖掘多维结构数据中独特的时空结构特征。本研究提出了一种刺激约束张量脑模型（STN），该模型涉及张量分解思想和刺激类别约束信息。该模型在真实的神经影像数据集（MEG 和 fMRI）上进行了验证。实验结果表明，STN模型在两个模态数据集上与其他方法相比，在准确率矩阵上取得了超过11.06%和18.46%的成绩。这些结果表明 STN 模型在提取判别特征方面的优越性，特别是在用语义信息解码对象刺激方面。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于张量分解的STN模型，用于从脑活动模式中识别外部刺激类别。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以有效提取脑网络中隐藏的多维结构信息，而张量分解能更好挖掘时空特征，因此需结合刺激类别约束改进模型。
AI.method: 提出STN模型，融合张量分解思想与刺激类别约束，通过实验证明其在MEG和fMRI数据集上的有效性。
AI.result: STN模型在两种模态数据集上的准确率分别提升11.06%和18.46%，验证了其提取判别特征的优势。
AI.conclusion: STN模型通过张量分解与刺激约束的结合，在解码含语义信息的对象刺激任务中表现优异。

search_text: STN: a new tensor network method to identify stimulus category from brain activity pattern STN：一种新的张量网络方法，用于从大脑活动模式中识别刺激类别 Neural decoding is still a challenge and hot topic in neurocomputing science. Recently, many studies have shown that brain network patterns containing rich spatial and temporal structure information, which represents the activation information of brain under external stimuli. %Therefore, the research of decoding stimuli from brain network received extensive more attention. The traditional method extracts brain network features directly from the common machine learning method, then puts these features into the classifier, and realizes to decode external stimuli. However, this method cannot effectively extract the multi-dimensional structural information, which is hidden in the brain network. The tensor researchers show that the tensor decomposition model can fully mine unique spatio-temporal structure characteristics in multi-dimensional structure data. This research proposed a stimulus constrained tensor brain model(STN)which involves the tensor decomposition idea and stimulus category constraint information. The model was verified on the real neuroimaging data sets (MEG and fMRI). The experimental results show that the STN model achieves more than 11.06% and 18.46% on accuracy matrix compared with others methods on two modal data sets. These results imply the superiority of extracting discriminative characteristics about STN model, especially for decoding object stimuli with semantic information. 神经解码仍然是神经计算科学中的挑战和热门话题。近年来，许多研究表明，大脑网络模式包含丰富的时空结构信息，代表了大脑在外界刺激下的激活信息。 %因此，从大脑网络解码刺激的研究受到了广泛的关注。传统方法直接从常见的机器学习方法中提取脑网络特征，然后将这些特征放入分类器中，实现对外部刺激的解码。然而，该方法无法有效提取隐藏在大脑网络中的多维结构信息。张量研究人员表明，张量分解模型可以充分挖掘多维结构数据中独特的时空结构特征。本研究提出了一种刺激约束张量脑模型（STN），该模型涉及张量分解思想和刺激类别约束信息。该模型在真实的神经影像数据集（MEG 和 fMRI）上进行了验证。实验结果表明，STN模型在两个模态数据集上与其他方法相比，在准确率矩阵上取得了超过11.06%和18.46%的成绩。这些结果表明 STN 模型在提取判别特征方面的优越性，特别是在用语义信息解码对象刺激方面。 本文提出了一种基于张量分解的STN模型，用于从脑活动模式中识别外部刺激类别。 Chunyu Liu,Jiacai Zhang q-bio.NC,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: S P Sharan,Wenqing Zheng,Kuo-Feng Hsu,Jiarong Xing,Ang Chen,Zhangyang Wang
title: Symbolic Distillation for Learned TCP Congestion Control
title_zh: 学习 TCP 拥塞控制的符号蒸馏
comment: Accepted in Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), 2022
comments: Accepted in Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), 2022
abstract: Recent advances in TCP congestion control (CC) have achieved tremendous success with deep reinforcement learning (RL) approaches, which use feedforward neural networks (NN) to learn complex environment conditions and make better decisions. However, such "black-box" policies lack interpretability and reliability, and often, they need to operate outside the traditional TCP datapath due to the use of complex NNs. This paper proposes a novel two-stage solution to achieve the best of both worlds: first to train a deep RL agent, then distill its (over-)parameterized NN policy into white-box, light-weight rules in the form of symbolic expressions that are much easier to understand and to implement in constrained environments. At the core of our proposal is a novel symbolic branching algorithm that enables the rule to be aware of the context in terms of various network conditions, eventually converting the NN policy into a symbolic tree. The distilled symbolic rules preserve and often improve performance over state-of-the-art NN policies while being faster and simpler than a standard neural network. We validate the performance of our distilled symbolic rules on both simulation and emulation environments. Our code is available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/SymbolicPCC.
summary: Recent advances in TCP congestion control (CC) have achieved tremendous success with deep reinforcement learning (RL) approaches, which use feedforward neural networks (NN) to learn complex environment conditions and make better decisions. However, such "black-box" policies lack interpretability and reliability, and often, they need to operate outside the traditional TCP datapath due to the use of complex NNs. This paper proposes a novel two-stage solution to achieve the best of both worlds: first to train a deep RL agent, then distill its (over-)parameterized NN policy into white-box, light-weight rules in the form of symbolic expressions that are much easier to understand and to implement in constrained environments. At the core of our proposal is a novel symbolic branching algorithm that enables the rule to be aware of the context in terms of various network conditions, eventually converting the NN policy into a symbolic tree. The distilled symbolic rules preserve and often improve performance over state-of-the-art NN policies while being faster and simpler than a standard neural network. We validate the performance of our distilled symbolic rules on both simulation and emulation environments. Our code is available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/SymbolicPCC.
abstract_zh: TCP 拥塞控制 (CC) 的最新进展通过深度强化学习 (RL) 方法取得了巨大成功，该方法使用前馈神经网络 (NN) 来学习复杂的环境条件并做出更好的决策。然而，这种“黑匣子”策略缺乏可解释性和可靠性，并且由于使用了复杂的神经网络，它们通常需要在传统 TCP 数据路径之外运行。本文提出了一种新颖的两阶段解决方案，以实现两全其美：首先训练深度强化学习代理，然后将其（过度）参数化的神经网络策略提炼为符号表达式形式的白盒轻量级规则，这些规则更容易理解并在受限环境中实现。我们提案的核心是一种新颖的符号分支算法，该算法使规则能够了解各种网络条件的上下文，最终将神经网络策略转换为符号树。提炼的符号规则保留并通常提高了最先进的神经网络策略的性能，同时比标准神经网络更快、更简单。我们验证了我们提炼的符号规则在模拟和仿真环境中的性能。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/VITA-Group/SymbolicPCC 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种两阶段方法，通过将深度强化学习训练的TCP拥塞控制策略蒸馏为可解释的符号规则，实现高性能与可解释性的结合。
AI.motivation: 现有基于深度强化学习的TCP拥塞控制策略存在黑箱特性、可解释性差及实现复杂的问题，难以在受限环境中部署。
AI.method: 首先训练深度强化学习代理，随后通过新颖的符号分支算法将过参数化的神经网络策略蒸馏为基于网络状态的符号表达式树结构。
AI.result: 蒸馏后的符号规则在仿真和仿真环境中表现出与先进神经网络相当或更优的性能，且计算效率更高，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为TCP拥塞控制提供了兼顾性能与可解释性的新型解决方案，适用于资源受限的现实部署场景。

However, such "black-box" policies lack interpretability and reliability, and often, they need to operate outside the traditional TCP datapath due to the use of complex NNs. This paper proposes a novel two-stage solution to achieve the best of both worlds: first to train a deep RL agent, then distill its (over-)parameterized NN policy into white-box, light-weight rules in the form of symbolic expressions that are much easier to understand and to implement in constrained environments. At the core of our proposal is a novel symbolic branching algorithm that enables the rule to be aware of the context in terms of various network conditions, eventually converting the NN policy into a symbolic tree. The distilled symbolic rules preserve and often improve performance over state-of-the-art NN policies while being faster and simpler than a standard neural network. We validate the performance of our distilled symbolic rules on both simulation and emulation environments. Our code is available at https://github.com/VITA-Group/SymbolicPCC. authors_text: Jun Zhou,Feng Qi,Zhigang Hua,Daohong Jian,Ziqi Liu,Hua Wu,Xingwen Zhang,Shuang Yang
title: A Practical Distributed ADMM Solver for Billion-Scale Generalized Assignment Problems
title_zh: 针对十亿级广义分配问题的实用分布式 ADMM 求解器
abstract: Assigning items to owners is a common problem found in various real-world applications, for example, audience-channel matching in marketing campaigns, borrower-lender matching in loan management, and shopper-merchant matching in e-commerce. Given an objective and multiple constraints, an assignment problem can be formulated as a constrained optimization problem. Such assignment problems are usually NP-hard, so when the number of items or the number of owners is large, solving for exact solutions becomes challenging. In this paper, we are interested in solving constrained assignment problems with hundreds of millions of items. Thus, with just tens of owners, the number of decision variables is at billion-scale. This scale is usually seen in the internet industry, which makes decisions for large groups of users. We relax the possible integer constraint, and formulate a general optimization problem that covers commonly seen assignment problems. Its objective function is convex. Its constraints are either linear, or convex and separable by items. We study to solve our generalized assignment problems in the Bregman Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (BADMM) framework where we exploit Bregman divergence to transform the Augmented Lagrangian into a separable form, and solve many subproblems in parallel. The entire solution can thus be implemented using a MapReduce-style distributed computation framework. We present experiment results on both synthetic and real-world datasets to verify its accuracy and scalability.
summary: Assigning items to owners is a common problem found in various real-world applications, for example, audience-channel matching in marketing campaigns, borrower-lender matching in loan management, and shopper-merchant matching in e-commerce. Given an objective and multiple constraints, an assignment problem can be formulated as a constrained optimization problem. Such assignment problems are usually NP-hard, so when the number of items or the number of owners is large, solving for exact solutions becomes challenging. In this paper, we are interested in solving constrained assignment problems with hundreds of millions of items. Thus, with just tens of owners, the number of decision variables is at billion-scale. This scale is usually seen in the internet industry, which makes decisions for large groups of users. We relax the possible integer constraint, and formulate a general optimization problem that covers commonly seen assignment problems. Its objective function is convex. Its constraints are either linear, or convex and separable by items. We study to solve our generalized assignment problems in the Bregman Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (BADMM) framework where we exploit Bregman divergence to transform the Augmented Lagrangian into a separable form, and solve many subproblems in parallel. The entire solution can thus be implemented using a MapReduce-style distributed computation framework. We present experiment results on both synthetic and real-world datasets to verify its accuracy and scalability.
abstract_zh: 将物品分配给所有者是各种现实应用中的常见问题，例如营销活动中的受众渠道匹配、贷款管理中的借款人-贷款人匹配以及电子商务中的购物者-商家匹配。给定目标和多个约束，分配问题可以表示为约束优化问题。此类分配问题通常是 NP 困难的，因此当项目数量或所有者数量很大时，求解精确解就变得具有挑战性。在本文中，我们感兴趣的是解决数亿个项目的约束分配问题。因此，在几十个业主的情况下，决策变量的数量就达到了十亿级。这种规模通常出现在互联网行业，因为互联网行业为大量用户做出决策。我们放宽了可能的整数约束，并制定了一个涵盖常见分配问题的通用优化问题。其目标函数是凸的。它的约束要么是线性的，要么是凸的，并且可以按项目分离。我们研究在布雷格曼交替方向乘子法（BADMM）框架中解决广义分配问题，其中我们利用布雷格曼散度将增广拉格朗日变换为可分离形式，并并行解决许多子问题。因此，整个解决方案可以使用 MapReduce 风格的分布式计算框架来实现。我们在合成数据集和真实数据集上提供了实验结果，以验证其准确性和可扩展性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于BADMM框架的分布式求解器，用于解决百亿规模的广义分配问题。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中的分配问题（如广告投放、借贷匹配）规模庞大且NP难，传统方法难以处理百亿级变量规模的优化问题。
AI.method: 通过Bregman散度将增广拉格朗日函数转化为可分形式，结合MapReduce框架实现并行求解，放松整数约束后构建凸优化模型。
AI.result: 在合成数据和真实数据集上验证了方法的准确性与可扩展性，能够高效处理大规模分配问题。
AI.conclusion: 提出的分布式BADMM框架为互联网行业的大规模用户决策问题提供了高效求解方案。
