authors_text: Bach Tran,Binh-Son Hua,Anh Tuan Tran,Minh Hoai
title: Self-Supervised Learning with Multi-View Rendering for 3D Point Cloud Analysis
title_zh: 用于 3D 点云分析的多视图渲染自监督学习
comment: ACCV 2022 paper. 14 pages of content, 4 pages of references, 6 pages of supplementary material
comments: ACCV 2022 paper. 14 pages of content, 4 pages of references, 6 pages of supplementary material
abstract: Recently, great progress has been made in 3D deep learning with the emergence of deep neural networks specifically designed for 3D point clouds. These networks are often trained from scratch or from pre-trained models learned purely from point cloud data. Inspired by the success of deep learning in the image domain, we devise a novel pre-training technique for better model initialization by utilizing the multi-view rendering of the 3D data. Our pre-training is self-supervised by a local pixel/point level correspondence loss computed from perspective projection and a global image/point cloud level loss based on knowledge distillation, thus effectively improving upon popular point cloud networks, including PointNet, DGCNN and SR-UNet. These improved models outperform existing state-of-the-art methods on various datasets and downstream tasks. We also analyze the benefits of synthetic and real data for pre-training, and observe that pre-training on synthetic data is also useful for high-level downstream tasks. Code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/VinAIResearch/selfsup_pcd.
summary: Recently, great progress has been made in 3D deep learning with the emergence of deep neural networks specifically designed for 3D point clouds. These networks are often trained from scratch or from pre-trained models learned purely from point cloud data. Inspired by the success of deep learning in the image domain, we devise a novel pre-training technique for better model initialization by utilizing the multi-view rendering of the 3D data. Our pre-training is self-supervised by a local pixel/point level correspondence loss computed from perspective projection and a global image/point cloud level loss based on knowledge distillation, thus effectively improving upon popular point cloud networks, including PointNet, DGCNN and SR-UNet. These improved models outperform existing state-of-the-art methods on various datasets and downstream tasks. We also analyze the benefits of synthetic and real data for pre-training, and observe that pre-training on synthetic data is also useful for high-level downstream tasks. Code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/VinAIResearch/selfsup_pcd.
abstract_zh: 最近，随着专门为 3D 点云设计的深度神经网络的出现，3D 深度学习取得了巨大进展。这些网络通常是从头开始训练的，或者是通过纯粹从点云数据学习的预训练模型进行训练的。受到图像领域深度学习成功的启发，我们设计了一种新颖的预训练技术，通过利用 3D 数据的多视图渲染来更好地进行模型初始化。我们的预训练是通过透视投影计算的局部像素/点级对应损失和基于知识蒸馏的全局图像/点云级损失进行自我监督，从而有效地改进了流行的点云网络，包括PointNet、DGCNN和SR-UNet。这些改进的模型在各种数据集和下游任务上优于现有的最先进的方法。我们还分析了合成数据和真实数据对预训练的好处，并观察到合成数据的预训练对于高级下游任务也很有用。代码和预训练模型可在 https://github.com/VinAIResearch/selfsup_pcd 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多视角渲染的自监督预训练方法，用于提升3D点云分析模型的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有3D点云网络多从头训练或仅依赖点云数据预训练，缺乏图像领域中自监督学习的高效初始化方法。
AI.method: 通过多视角渲染生成图像，结合局部像素/点对应损失与全局知识蒸馏损失，实现点云模型的自监督预训练。
AI.result: 改进后的PointNet、DGCNN和SR-UNet在多个数据集及下游任务中超越现有方法，且合成数据预训练对高层任务有效。
AI.conclusion: 多视角渲染自监督预训练显著提升3D点云模型性能，合成数据在预训练中具有实用价值。

search_text: Self-Supervised Learning with Multi-View Rendering for 3D Point Cloud Analysis 用于 3D 点云分析的多视图渲染自监督学习 Recently, great progress has been made in 3D deep learning with the emergence of deep neural networks specifically designed for 3D point clouds. These networks are often trained from scratch or from pre-trained models learned purely from point cloud data. Inspired by the success of deep learning in the image domain, we devise a novel pre-training technique for better model initialization by utilizing the multi-view rendering of the 3D data. Our pre-training is self-supervised by a local pixel/point level correspondence loss computed from perspective projection and a global image/point cloud level loss based on knowledge distillation, thus effectively improving upon popular point cloud networks, including PointNet, DGCNN and SR-UNet. These improved models outperform existing state-of-the-art methods on various datasets and downstream tasks. We also analyze the benefits of synthetic and real data for pre-training, and observe that pre-training on synthetic data is also useful for high-level downstream tasks. Code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/VinAIResearch/selfsup_pcd. 最近，随着专门为 3D 点云设计的深度神经网络的出现，3D 深度学习取得了巨大进展。这些网络通常是从头开始训练的，或者是通过纯粹从点云数据学习的预训练模型进行训练的。受到图像领域深度学习成功的启发，我们设计了一种新颖的预训练技术，通过利用 3D 数据的多视图渲染来更好地进行模型初始化。我们的预训练是通过透视投影计算的局部像素/点级对应损失和基于知识蒸馏的全局图像/点云级损失进行自我监督，从而有效地改进了流行的点云网络，包括PointNet、DGCNN和SR-UNet。这些改进的模型在各种数据集和下游任务上优于现有的最先进的方法。我们还分析了合成数据和真实数据对预训练的好处，并观察到合成数据的预训练对于高级下游任务也很有用。代码和预训练模型可在 https://github.com/VinAIResearch/selfsup_pcd 获取。 本文提出一种基于多视角渲染的自监督预训练方法，用于提升3D点云分析模型的性能。 Bach Tran,Binh-Son Hua,Anh Tuan Tran,Minh Hoai cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.GR

authors_text: Ruijie Tao,Kong Aik Lee,Zhan Shi,Haizhou Li
title: Speaker recognition with two-step multi-modal deep cleansing
title_zh: 两步多模式深度清洁的说话人识别
abstract: Neural network-based speaker recognition has achieved significant improvement in recent years. A robust speaker representation learns meaningful knowledge from both hard and easy samples in the training set to achieve good performance. However, noisy samples (i.e., with wrong labels) in the training set induce confusion and cause the network to learn the incorrect representation. In this paper, we propose a two-step audio-visual deep cleansing framework to eliminate the effect of noisy labels in speaker representation learning. This framework contains a coarse-grained cleansing step to search for the peculiar samples, followed by a fine-grained cleansing step to filter out the noisy labels. Our study starts from an efficient audio-visual speaker recognition system, which achieves a close to perfect equal-error-rate (EER) of 0.01\%, 0.07\% and 0.13\% on the Vox-O, E and H test sets. With the proposed multi-modal cleansing mechanism, four different speaker recognition networks achieve an average improvement of 5.9\%. Code has been made available at: \textcolor{magenta}{\url{https://github.com/TaoRuijie/AVCleanse}}.
summary: Neural network-based speaker recognition has achieved significant improvement in recent years. A robust speaker representation learns meaningful knowledge from both hard and easy samples in the training set to achieve good performance. However, noisy samples (i.e., with wrong labels) in the training set induce confusion and cause the network to learn the incorrect representation. In this paper, we propose a two-step audio-visual deep cleansing framework to eliminate the effect of noisy labels in speaker representation learning. This framework contains a coarse-grained cleansing step to search for the peculiar samples, followed by a fine-grained cleansing step to filter out the noisy labels. Our study starts from an efficient audio-visual speaker recognition system, which achieves a close to perfect equal-error-rate (EER) of 0.01\%, 0.07\% and 0.13\% on the Vox-O, E and H test sets. With the proposed multi-modal cleansing mechanism, four different speaker recognition networks achieve an average improvement of 5.9\%. Code has been made available at: \textcolor{magenta}{\url{https://github.com/TaoRuijie/AVCleanse}}.
abstract_zh: 基于神经网络的说话人识别近年来取得了显着的进步。强大的说话人表示可以从训练集中的困难样本和简单样本中学习有意义的知识，以实现良好的性能。然而，训练集中的噪声样本（即带有错误标签）会引起混乱并导致网络学习不正确的表示。在本文中，我们提出了一种两步视听深度清理框架，以消除说话人表示学习中噪声标签的影响。该框架包含一个粗粒度的清理步骤来搜索特殊样本，然后是一个细粒度的清理步骤来过滤掉噪声标签。我们的研究从高效的视听说话人识别系统开始，该系统在 Vox-O、E 和 H 测试集上实现了接近完美的等错误率 (EER) 0.01\%、0.07\% 和 0.13\%。通过所提出的多模式清理机制，四种不同的说话人识别网络平均提高了 5.9%。代码已提供于：\textcolor{magenta}{\url{https://github.com/TaoRuijie/AVCleanse}}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种两步多模态深度清洗框架，用于消除说话人识别中训练集噪声标签的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络说话人识别方法受训练集中噪声样本（错误标签）干扰，导致学习到错误的表示，影响识别性能。
AI.method: 设计包含粗粒度清洗（识别异常样本）和细粒度清洗（过滤噪声标签）的两阶段音频-视觉清洗框架，结合多模态信息提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在Vox-O/E/H测试集上实现接近完美的等错误率（0.01%/0.07%/0.13%），四个模型平均提升5.9%。
AI.conclusion: 多模态深度清洗机制有效缓解噪声标签干扰，显著提升说话人识别系统的准确性和稳定性。

authors_text: Qichao Ying,Hang Zhou,Zhenxing Qian,Sheng Li,Xinpeng Zhang
title: Learning to Immunize Images for Tamper Localization and Self-Recovery
title_zh: 学习免疫图像以进行篡改定位和自我恢复
comment: Under Review. Extended version of our ACMMM 2021 paper
comments: Under Review. Extended version of our ACMMM 2021 paper
abstract: Digital images are vulnerable to nefarious tampering attacks such as content addition or removal that severely alter the original meaning. It is somehow like a person without protection that is open to various kinds of viruses. Image immunization (Imuge) is a technology of protecting the images by introducing trivial perturbation, so that the protected images are immune to the viruses in that the tampered contents can be auto-recovered. This paper presents Imuge+, an enhanced scheme for image immunization. By observing the invertible relationship between image immunization and the corresponding self-recovery, we employ an invertible neural network to jointly learn image immunization and recovery respectively in the forward and backward pass. We also introduce an efficient attack layer that involves both malicious tamper and benign image post-processing, where a novel distillation-based JPEG simulator is proposed for improved JPEG robustness. Our method achieves promising results in real-world tests where experiments show accurate tamper localization as well as high-fidelity content recovery. Additionally, we show superior performance on tamper localization compared to state-of-the-art schemes based on passive forensics.
summary: Digital images are vulnerable to nefarious tampering attacks such as content addition or removal that severely alter the original meaning. It is somehow like a person without protection that is open to various kinds of viruses. Image immunization (Imuge) is a technology of protecting the images by introducing trivial perturbation, so that the protected images are immune to the viruses in that the tampered contents can be auto-recovered. This paper presents Imuge+, an enhanced scheme for image immunization. By observing the invertible relationship between image immunization and the corresponding self-recovery, we employ an invertible neural network to jointly learn image immunization and recovery respectively in the forward and backward pass. We also introduce an efficient attack layer that involves both malicious tamper and benign image post-processing, where a novel distillation-based JPEG simulator is proposed for improved JPEG robustness. Our method achieves promising results in real-world tests where experiments show accurate tamper localization as well as high-fidelity content recovery. Additionally, we show superior performance on tamper localization compared to state-of-the-art schemes based on passive forensics.
abstract_zh: 数字图像容易受到恶意篡改攻击，例如内容添加或删除，从而严重改变原始含义。这就像一个没有保护的人容易受到各种病毒的侵害。图像免疫（Imuge）是一种通过引入微小扰动来保护图像的技术，使受保护的图像免受病毒的侵害，被篡改的内容可以自动恢复。本文提出了 Imuge+，一种图像免疫的增强方案。通过观察图像免疫和相应的自恢复之间的可逆关系，我们采用可逆神经网络分别在前向和后向传递中联合学习图像免疫和恢复。我们还引入了一个有效的攻击层，涉及恶意篡改和良性图像后处理，其中提出了一种新颖的基于蒸馏的 JPEG 模拟器，以提高 JPEG 的鲁棒性。我们的方法在现实世界的测试中取得了有希望的结果，实验显示了准确的篡改定位以及高保真内容恢复。此外，与基于被动取证的最先进方案相比，我们在篡改定位方面表现出了卓越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Imuge+方法，通过引入可逆神经网络和攻击层，实现图像的防篡改与自恢复。
AI.motivation: 数字图像易受恶意篡改，现有方法在真实场景中鲁棒性不足，需提升篡改定位与内容恢复能力。
AI.method: 利用可逆神经网络联合学习图像免疫与恢复，设计包含恶意篡改和良性后处理的攻击层，并提出基于知识蒸馏的JPEG模拟器增强鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在真实场景测试中实现精准篡改定位与高保真恢复，篡改检测性能优于基于被动取证的最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: Imuge+通过可逆学习框架和攻击模拟层，有效提升了图像免疫系统的实用性与抗攻击能力。

tldr: 本文提出Imuge+方法，通过引入可逆神经网络和攻击层，实现图像的防篡改与自恢复。
motivation: 数字图像易受恶意篡改，现有方法在真实场景中鲁棒性不足，需提升篡改定位与内容恢复能力。
method: 利用可逆神经网络联合学习图像免疫与恢复，设计包含恶意篡改和良性后处理的攻击层，并提出基于知识蒸馏的JPEG模拟器增强鲁棒性。
result: 在真实场景测试中实现精准篡改定位与高保真恢复，篡改检测性能优于基于被动取证的最先进方法。
conclusion: Imuge+通过可逆学习框架和攻击模拟层，有效提升了图像免疫系统的实用性与抗攻击能力。
search_text: Learning to Immunize Images for Tamper Localization and Self-Recovery 学习免疫图像以进行篡改定位和自我恢复 Digital images are vulnerable to nefarious tampering attacks such as content addition or removal that severely alter the original meaning. It is somehow like a person without protection that is open to various kinds of viruses. Image immunization (Imuge) is a technology of protecting the images by introducing trivial perturbation, so that the protected images are immune to the viruses in that the tampered contents can be auto-recovered. This paper presents Imuge+, an enhanced scheme for image immunization. By observing the invertible relationship between image immunization and the corresponding self-recovery, we employ an invertible neural network to jointly learn image immunization and recovery respectively in the forward and backward pass. We also introduce an efficient attack layer that involves both malicious tamper and benign image post-processing, where a novel distillation-based JPEG simulator is proposed for improved JPEG robustness. Our method achieves promising results in real-world tests where experiments show accurate tamper localization as well as high-fidelity content recovery. Additionally, we show superior performance on tamper localization compared to state-of-the-art schemes based on passive forensics. 数字图像容易受到恶意篡改攻击，例如内容添加或删除，从而严重改变原始含义。这就像一个没有保护的人容易受到各种病毒的侵害。图像免疫（Imuge）是一种通过引入微小扰动来保护图像的技术，使受保护的图像免受病毒的侵害，被篡改的内容可以自动恢复。本文提出了 Imuge+，一种图像免疫的增强方案。通过观察图像免疫和相应的自恢复之间的可逆关系，我们采用可逆神经网络分别在前向和后向传递中联合学习图像免疫和恢复。我们还引入了一个有效的攻击层，涉及恶意篡改和良性图像后处理，其中提出了一种新颖的基于蒸馏的 JPEG 模拟器，以提高 JPEG 的鲁棒性。我们的方法在现实世界的测试中取得了有希望的结果，实验显示了准确的篡改定位以及高保真内容恢复。此外，与基于被动取证的最先进方案相比，我们在篡改定位方面表现出了卓越的性能。 本文提出Imuge+方法，通过引入可逆神经网络和攻击层，实现图像的防篡改与自恢复。 Qichao Ying,Hang Zhou,Zhenxing Qian,Sheng Li,Xinpeng Zhang eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Phuoc-Hieu Le,Quynh Le,Rang Nguyen,Binh-Son Hua
title: Single-Image HDR Reconstruction by Multi-Exposure Generation
title_zh: 通过多重曝光生成进行单图像 HDR 重建
comment: WACV 2023 paper. 8 pages of content, 2 pages of references, 8 pages of supplementary material
comments: WACV 2023 paper. 8 pages of content, 2 pages of references, 8 pages of supplementary material
abstract: High dynamic range (HDR) imaging is an indispensable technique in modern photography. Traditional methods focus on HDR reconstruction from multiple images, solving the core problems of image alignment, fusion, and tone mapping, yet having a perfect solution due to ghosting and other visual artifacts in the reconstruction. Recent attempts at single-image HDR reconstruction show a promising alternative: by learning to map pixel values to their irradiance using a neural network, one can bypass the align-and-merge pipeline completely yet still obtain a high-quality HDR image. In this work, we propose a weakly supervised learning method that inverts the physical image formation process for HDR reconstruction via learning to generate multiple exposures from a single image. Our neural network can invert the camera response to reconstruct pixel irradiance before synthesizing multiple exposures and hallucinating details in under- and over-exposed regions from a single input image. To train the network, we propose a representation loss, a reconstruction loss, and a perceptual loss applied on pairs of under- and over-exposure images and thus do not require HDR images for training. Our experiments show that our proposed model can effectively reconstruct HDR images. Our qualitative and quantitative results show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the DrTMO dataset. Our code is available at https://github.com/VinAIResearch/single_image_hdr.
summary: High dynamic range (HDR) imaging is an indispensable technique in modern photography. Traditional methods focus on HDR reconstruction from multiple images, solving the core problems of image alignment, fusion, and tone mapping, yet having a perfect solution due to ghosting and other visual artifacts in the reconstruction. Recent attempts at single-image HDR reconstruction show a promising alternative: by learning to map pixel values to their irradiance using a neural network, one can bypass the align-and-merge pipeline completely yet still obtain a high-quality HDR image. In this work, we propose a weakly supervised learning method that inverts the physical image formation process for HDR reconstruction via learning to generate multiple exposures from a single image. Our neural network can invert the camera response to reconstruct pixel irradiance before synthesizing multiple exposures and hallucinating details in under- and over-exposed regions from a single input image. To train the network, we propose a representation loss, a reconstruction loss, and a perceptual loss applied on pairs of under- and over-exposure images and thus do not require HDR images for training. Our experiments show that our proposed model can effectively reconstruct HDR images. Our qualitative and quantitative results show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the DrTMO dataset. Our code is available at https://github.com/VinAIResearch/single_image_hdr.
abstract_zh: 高动态范围（HDR）成像是现代摄影中不可或缺的技术。传统方法侧重于多幅图像的 HDR 重建，解决了图像对齐、融合和色调映射的核心问题，但由于重建中的重影和其他视觉伪影而得到了完美的解决。最近对单图像 HDR 重建的尝试显示了一种有前途的替代方案：通过学习使用神经网络将像素值映射到其辐照度，人们可以完全绕过对齐和合并管道，但仍然可以获得高质量的 HDR 图像。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种弱监督学习方法，通过学习从单个图像生成多次曝光来反转 HDR 重建的物理图像形成过程。我们的神经网络可以反转相机响应以重建像素辐照度，然后合成多个曝光并从单个输入图像中产生曝光不足和过度曝光区域的幻觉细节。为了训练网络，我们提出了应用于曝光不足和过度曝光图像对的表示损失、重建损失和感知损失，因此不需要 HDR 图像进行训练。我们的实验表明，我们提出的模型可以有效地重建 HDR 图像。我们的定性和定量结果表明，我们的方法在 DrTMO 数据集上实现了最先进的性能。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/VinAIResearch/single_image_hdr 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于弱监督学习的单图像HDR重建方法，通过生成多曝光图像实现高质量HDR重建。
AI.motivation: 传统多图像HDR重建存在鬼影等伪影问题，而现有单图像方法难以准确还原辐照度信息，需解决无需HDR训练数据的重建难题。
AI.method: 设计弱监督学习框架，逆向相机响应过程生成多曝光图像，结合表示损失、重建损失和感知损失进行训练，无需HDR真值数据。
AI.result: 在DrTMO数据集上实现SOTA性能，定性定量结果验证了方法有效性，生成HDR图像细节丰富且无明显伪影。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过生成多曝光图像有效解决了单图像HDR重建问题，在无需HDR训练数据条件下达到先进水平。

authors_text: Weiyan Shi,Emily Dinan,Kurt Shuster,Jason Weston,Jing Xu
title: When Life Gives You Lemons, Make Cherryade: Converting Feedback from Bad Responses into Good Labels
title_zh: 当生活给你柠檬时，制作樱桃汽水：将不良反应的反馈转化为良好的标签
abstract: Deployed dialogue agents have the potential to integrate human feedback to continuously improve themselves. However, humans may not always provide explicit signals when the chatbot makes mistakes during interactions. In this work, we propose Juicer, a framework to make use of both binary and free-form textual human feedback. It works by: (i) extending sparse binary feedback by training a satisfaction classifier to label the unlabeled data; and (ii) training a reply corrector to map the bad replies to good ones. We find that augmenting training with model-corrected replies improves the final dialogue model, and we can further improve performance by using both positive and negative replies through the recently proposed Director model.
summary: Deployed dialogue agents have the potential to integrate human feedback to continuously improve themselves. However, humans may not always provide explicit signals when the chatbot makes mistakes during interactions. In this work, we propose Juicer, a framework to make use of both binary and free-form textual human feedback. It works by: (i) extending sparse binary feedback by training a satisfaction classifier to label the unlabeled data; and (ii) training a reply corrector to map the bad replies to good ones. We find that augmenting training with model-corrected replies improves the final dialogue model, and we can further improve performance by using both positive and negative replies through the recently proposed Director model.
abstract_zh: 部署的对话代理有潜力整合人类反馈来不断改进自己。然而，当聊天机器人在交互过程中犯错时，人类可能并不总是提供明确的信号。在这项工作中，我们提出了 Juicer，一个利用二进制和自由格式文本人类反馈的框架。它的工作原理是：（i）通过训练满意度分类器来标记未标记的数据来扩展稀疏二进制反馈； (ii) 训练回复校正器将不好的回复映射到好的回复。我们发现，通过模型校正的回复来增强训练可以改善最终的对话模型，并且我们可以通过最近提出的Director模型使用正面和负面的回复来进一步提高性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Juicer框架，通过将人类反馈中的负面回复转化为有效标签，提升对话代理的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有对话系统难以有效利用用户稀疏或隐式的负面反馈，导致模型改进受限。
AI.method: 构建两阶段框架：1) 训练满意度分类器扩展二元反馈；2) 训练回复校正器将坏回复映射为好回复，并结合Director模型利用正负样本。
AI.result: 实验表明，使用校正后回复增强训练可提升对话模型效果，结合正负回复进一步优化性能。
AI.conclusion: Juicer框架有效转化人类反馈为高质量训练数据，为对话系统持续学习提供新方法。

authors_text: Wenguan Wang,Yi Yang,Fei Wu
title: Towards Data-and Knowledge-Driven Artificial Intelligence: A Survey on Neuro-Symbolic Computing
title_zh: 迈向数据和知识驱动的人工智能：神经符号计算调查
abstract: Neural-symbolic computing (NeSy), which pursues the integration of the symbolic and statistical paradigms of cognition, has been an active research area of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for many years. As NeSy shows promise of reconciling the advantages of reasoning and interpretability of symbolic representation and robust learning in neural networks, it may serve as a catalyst for the next generation of AI. In the present paper, we provide a systematic overview of the recent developments and important contributions of NeSy research. Firstly, we introduce study history of this area, covering early work and foundations. We further discuss background concepts and identify key driving factors behind the development of NeSy. Afterward, we categorize recent landmark approaches along several main characteristics that underline this research paradigm, including neural-symbolic integration, knowledge representation, knowledge embedding, and functionality. Next, we briefly discuss the successful application of modern NeSy approaches in several domains. Then, we benchmark several NeSy methods on three representative application tasks. Finally, we identify the open problems together with potential future research directions. This survey is expected to help new researchers enter this rapidly evolving field and accelerate the progress towards data-and knowledge-driven AI.
summary: Neural-symbolic computing (NeSy), which pursues the integration of the symbolic and statistical paradigms of cognition, has been an active research area of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for many years. As NeSy shows promise of reconciling the advantages of reasoning and interpretability of symbolic representation and robust learning in neural networks, it may serve as a catalyst for the next generation of AI. In the present paper, we provide a systematic overview of the recent developments and important contributions of NeSy research. Firstly, we introduce study history of this area, covering early work and foundations. We further discuss background concepts and identify key driving factors behind the development of NeSy. Afterward, we categorize recent landmark approaches along several main characteristics that underline this research paradigm, including neural-symbolic integration, knowledge representation, knowledge embedding, and functionality. Next, we briefly discuss the successful application of modern NeSy approaches in several domains. Then, we benchmark several NeSy methods on three representative application tasks. Finally, we identify the open problems together with potential future research directions. This survey is expected to help new researchers enter this rapidly evolving field and accelerate the progress towards data-and knowledge-driven AI.
abstract_zh: 神经符号计算（NeSy）追求认知的符号和统计范式的整合，多年来一直是人工智能（AI）的活跃研究领域。由于 NeSy 有望调和符号表示的推理和可解释性以及神经网络中稳健学习的优势，因此它可能会成为下一代人工智能的催化剂。在本文中，我们系统地概述了 NeSy 研究的最新进展和重要贡献。首先，我们介绍该领域的研究历史，包括早期工作和基础。我们进一步讨论背景概念并确定 NeSy 开发背后的关键驱动因素。之后，我们根据强调该研究范式的几个主要特征对最近的里程碑式方法进行分类，包括神经符号集成、知识表示、知识嵌入和功能。接下来，我们简要讨论现代 NeSy 方法在几个领域的成功应用。然后，我们在三个代表性应用任务上对几种 NeSy 方法进行了基准测试。最后，我们确定了未解决的问题以及未来潜在的研究方向。这项调查预计将帮助新研究人员进入这个快速发展的领域，并加速数据和知识驱动的人工智能的进展。

AI.tldr: 系统综述神经符号计算（NeSy）领域的最新进展、方法分类、应用案例及未来方向，推动数据与知识驱动的AI发展。
AI.motivation: 解决传统AI方法在可解释性与学习能力之间的矛盾，推动更高效、可解释的AI系统发展。
AI.method: 通过历史回顾、概念分析、方法分类、应用案例分析及基准测试，系统梳理NeSy的研究进展。
AI.result: 总结NeSy的关键方法与应用，识别当前挑战，并提出未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 综述为研究者提供全面参考，促进数据与知识驱动的AI发展。

search_text: Towards Data-and Knowledge-Driven Artificial Intelligence: A Survey on Neuro-Symbolic Computing 迈向数据和知识驱动的人工智能：神经符号计算调查 Neural-symbolic computing (NeSy), which pursues the integration of the symbolic and statistical paradigms of cognition, has been an active research area of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for many years. As NeSy shows promise of reconciling the advantages of reasoning and interpretability of symbolic representation and robust learning in neural networks, it may serve as a catalyst for the next generation of AI. In the present paper, we provide a systematic overview of the recent developments and important contributions of NeSy research. Firstly, we introduce study history of this area, covering early work and foundations. We further discuss background concepts and identify key driving factors behind the development of NeSy. Afterward, we categorize recent landmark approaches along several main characteristics that underline this research paradigm, including neural-symbolic integration, knowledge representation, knowledge embedding, and functionality. Next, we briefly discuss the successful application of modern NeSy approaches in several domains. Then, we benchmark several NeSy methods on three representative application tasks. Finally, we identify the open problems together with potential future research directions. This survey is expected to help new researchers enter this rapidly evolving field and accelerate the progress towards data-and knowledge-driven AI. 神经符号计算（NeSy）追求认知的符号和统计范式的整合，多年来一直是人工智能（AI）的活跃研究领域。由于 NeSy 有望调和符号表示的推理和可解释性以及神经网络中稳健学习的优势，因此它可能会成为下一代人工智能的催化剂。在本文中，我们系统地概述了 NeSy 研究的最新进展和重要贡献。首先，我们介绍该领域的研究历史，包括早期工作和基础。我们进一步讨论背景概念并确定 NeSy 开发背后的关键驱动因素。之后，我们根据强调该研究范式的几个主要特征对最近的里程碑式方法进行分类，包括神经符号集成、知识表示、知识嵌入和功能。接下来，我们简要讨论现代 NeSy 方法在几个领域的成功应用。然后，我们在三个代表性应用任务上对几种 NeSy 方法进行了基准测试。最后，我们确定了未解决的问题以及未来潜在的研究方向。这项调查预计将帮助新研究人员进入这个快速发展的领域，并加速数据和知识驱动的人工智能的进展。 系统综述神经符号计算（NeSy）领域的最新进展、方法分类、应用案例及未来方向，推动数据与知识驱动的AI发展。 Wenguan Wang,Yi Yang,Fei Wu cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Reo Yoneyama,Ryuichi Yamamoto,Kentaro Tachibana
title: Nonparallel High-Quality Audio Super Resolution with Domain Adaptation and Resampling CycleGANs
title_zh: 具有域适应和重采样 CycleGAN 的非并行高质量音频超分辨率
abstract: Neural audio super-resolution models are typically trained on low- and high-resolution audio signal pairs. Although these methods achieve highly accurate super-resolution if the acoustic characteristics of the input data are similar to those of the training data, challenges remain: the models suffer from quality degradation for out-of-domain data, and paired data are required for training. To address these problems, we propose Dual-CycleGAN, a high-quality audio super-resolution method that can utilize unpaired data based on two connected cycle consistent generative adversarial networks (CycleGAN). Our method decomposes the super-resolution method into domain adaptation and resampling processes to handle acoustic mismatch in the unpaired low- and high-resolution signals. The two processes are then jointly optimized within the CycleGAN framework. Experimental results verify that the proposed method significantly outperforms conventional methods when paired data are not available. Code and audio samples are available from https://chomeyama.github.io/DualCycleGAN-Demo/.
summary: Neural audio super-resolution models are typically trained on low- and high-resolution audio signal pairs. Although these methods achieve highly accurate super-resolution if the acoustic characteristics of the input data are similar to those of the training data, challenges remain: the models suffer from quality degradation for out-of-domain data, and paired data are required for training. To address these problems, we propose Dual-CycleGAN, a high-quality audio super-resolution method that can utilize unpaired data based on two connected cycle consistent generative adversarial networks (CycleGAN). Our method decomposes the super-resolution method into domain adaptation and resampling processes to handle acoustic mismatch in the unpaired low- and high-resolution signals. The two processes are then jointly optimized within the CycleGAN framework. Experimental results verify that the proposed method significantly outperforms conventional methods when paired data are not available. Code and audio samples are available from https://chomeyama.github.io/DualCycleGAN-Demo/.
abstract_zh: 神经音频超分辨率模型通常在低分辨率和高分辨率音频信号对上进行训练。尽管如果输入数据的声学特性与训练数据的声学特性相似，这些方法可以实现高精度的超分辨率，但挑战仍然存在：模型会因域外数据而导致质量下降，并且需要配对数据进行训练。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了 Dual-CycleGAN，这是一种高质量的音频超分辨率方法，可以利用基于两个连接的循环一致生成对抗网络 (CycleGAN) 的不配对数据。我们的方法将超分辨率方法分解为域适应和重采样过程，以处理不成对的低分辨率和高分辨率信号中的声学失配。然后这两个过程在 CycleGAN 框架内联合优化。实验结果验证了当配对数据不可用时，所提出的方法显着优于传统方法。代码和音频示例可从 https://chomeyama.github.io/DualCycleGAN-Demo/ 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于域适应和重采样CycleGAN的非平行高质量音频超分辨率方法Dual-CycleGAN，解决无配对数据和域外数据质量下降的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统音频超分辨率模型依赖配对数据且在域外数据上表现不佳，需开发无需配对数据且能处理声学不匹配的新型方法。
AI.method: 通过连接两个循环一致的生成对抗网络（CycleGAN），将超分辨率分解为域适应和重采样过程，并在CycleGAN框架内联合优化。
AI.result: 实验验证在无配对数据情况下，该方法显著优于传统方法，代码和音频样本已公开。
AI.conclusion: Dual-CycleGAN有效利用无配对数据解决声学不匹配问题，为音频超分辨率提供高质量解决方案。

authors_text: Byung-Hak Kim,Zhongfen Deng,Philip S. Yu,Varun Ganapathi
title: Can Current Explainability Help Provide References in Clinical Notes to Support Humans Annotate Medical Codes?
title_zh: 当前的可解释性能否帮助在临床记录中提供参考以支持人类注释医疗代码？
comment: To appear in Proceedings of the 13th International Workshop on Health Text Mining and Information Analysis (Louhi 2022), Virtual, December 7, 2022
comments: To appear in Proceedings of the 13th International Workshop on Health Text Mining and Information Analysis (Louhi 2022), Virtual, December 7, 2022
abstract: The medical codes prediction problem from clinical notes has received substantial interest in the NLP community, and several recent studies have shown the state-of-the-art (SOTA) code prediction results of full-fledged deep learning-based methods. However, most previous SOTA works based on deep learning are still in early stages in terms of providing textual references and explanations of the predicted codes, despite the fact that this level of explainability of the prediction outcomes is critical to gaining trust from professional medical coders. This raises the important question of how well current explainability methods apply to advanced neural network models such as transformers to predict correct codes and present references in clinical notes that support code prediction. First, we present an explainable Read, Attend, and Code (xRAC) framework and assess two approaches, attention score-based xRAC-ATTN and model-agnostic knowledge-distillation-based xRAC-KD, through simplified but thorough human-grounded evaluations with SOTA transformer-based model, RAC. We find that the supporting evidence text highlighted by xRAC-ATTN is of higher quality than xRAC-KD whereas xRAC-KD has potential advantages in production deployment scenarios. More importantly, we show for the first time that, given the current state of explainability methodologies, using the SOTA medical codes prediction system still requires the expertise and competencies of professional coders, even though its prediction accuracy is superior to that of human coders. This, we believe, is a very meaningful step toward developing explainable and accurate machine learning systems for fully autonomous medical code prediction from clinical notes.
summary: The medical codes prediction problem from clinical notes has received substantial interest in the NLP community, and several recent studies have shown the state-of-the-art (SOTA) code prediction results of full-fledged deep learning-based methods. However, most previous SOTA works based on deep learning are still in early stages in terms of providing textual references and explanations of the predicted codes, despite the fact that this level of explainability of the prediction outcomes is critical to gaining trust from professional medical coders. This raises the important question of how well current explainability methods apply to advanced neural network models such as transformers to predict correct codes and present references in clinical notes that support code prediction. First, we present an explainable Read, Attend, and Code (xRAC) framework and assess two approaches, attention score-based xRAC-ATTN and model-agnostic knowledge-distillation-based xRAC-KD, through simplified but thorough human-grounded evaluations with SOTA transformer-based model, RAC. We find that the supporting evidence text highlighted by xRAC-ATTN is of higher quality than xRAC-KD whereas xRAC-KD has potential advantages in production deployment scenarios. More importantly, we show for the first time that, given the current state of explainability methodologies, using the SOTA medical codes prediction system still requires the expertise and competencies of professional coders, even though its prediction accuracy is superior to that of human coders. This, we believe, is a very meaningful step toward developing explainable and accurate machine learning systems for fully autonomous medical code prediction from clinical notes.
abstract_zh: 来自临床记录的医疗代码预测问题引起了 NLP 社区的极大兴趣，最近的几项研究已经展示了成熟的基于深度学习的方法的最先进 (SOTA) 代码预测结果。然而，大多数之前基于深度学习的 SOTA 工作在提供预测代码的文本参考和解释方面仍处于早期阶段，尽管预测结果的这种可解释性水平对于获得专业医疗编码人员的信任至关重要。这就提出了一个重要问题：当前的可解释性方法如何应用于高级神经网络模型（例如 Transformer）来预测正确的代码并在支持代码预测的临床记录中提供参考。首先，我们提出了一个可解释的阅读、参加和编码 (xRAC) 框架，并通过使用基于 SOTA 变压器的模型 RAC 进行简化但彻底的人性化评估，评估两种方法：基于注意力评分的 xRAC-ATTN 和基于模型无关知识蒸馏的 xRAC-KD。我们发现 xRAC-ATTN 突出显示的支持证据文本质量高于 xRAC-KD，而 xRAC-KD 在生产部署场景中具有潜在优势。更重要的是，我们首次表明，考虑到可解释性方法的当前状态，使用 SOTA 医疗代码预测系统仍然需要专业编码员的专业知识和能力，尽管其预测精度优于人类编码员。我们相信，这是朝着开发可解释且准确的机器学习系统迈出的非常有意义的一步，以便根据临床记录进行完全自主的医疗代码预测。

AI.tldr: 本文评估当前可解释性方法在临床笔记中为医学编码预测提供参考文本的能力。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度学习模型在医学编码预测中表现优异，但其可解释性不足，难以获得专业编码员信任。
AI.method: 提出可解释框架xRAC，包含基于注意力机制的xRAC-ATTN和知识蒸馏的xRAC-KD，并通过人类评估对比SOTA模型RAC。
AI.result: xRAC-ATTN生成的参考文本质量更高，而xRAC-KD更适合生产部署；即使使用顶级模型仍需专业编码员参与。
AI.conclusion: 当前可解释性方法不足以实现完全自主的医学编码预测，需进一步改进可解释性与准确性平衡的系统。

motivation: 尽管深度学习模型在医学编码预测中表现优异，但其可解释性不足，难以获得专业编码员信任。

search_text: Can Current Explainability Help Provide References in Clinical Notes to Support Humans Annotate Medical Codes? 当前的可解释性能否帮助在临床记录中提供参考以支持人类注释医疗代码？ The medical codes prediction problem from clinical notes has received substantial interest in the NLP community, and several recent studies have shown the state-of-the-art (SOTA) code prediction results of full-fledged deep learning-based methods. However, most previous SOTA works based on deep learning are still in early stages in terms of providing textual references and explanations of the predicted codes, despite the fact that this level of explainability of the prediction outcomes is critical to gaining trust from professional medical coders. This raises the important question of how well current explainability methods apply to advanced neural network models such as transformers to predict correct codes and present references in clinical notes that support code prediction. First, we present an explainable Read, Attend, and Code (xRAC) framework and assess two approaches, attention score-based xRAC-ATTN and model-agnostic knowledge-distillation-based xRAC-KD, through simplified but thorough human-grounded evaluations with SOTA transformer-based model, RAC. We find that the supporting evidence text highlighted by xRAC-ATTN is of higher quality than xRAC-KD whereas xRAC-KD has potential advantages in production deployment scenarios. More importantly, we show for the first time that, given the current state of explainability methodologies, using the SOTA medical codes prediction system still requires the expertise and competencies of professional coders, even though its prediction accuracy is superior to that of human coders. This, we believe, is a very meaningful step toward developing explainable and accurate machine learning systems for fully autonomous medical code prediction from clinical notes. 来自临床记录的医疗代码预测问题引起了 NLP 社区的极大兴趣，最近的几项研究已经展示了成熟的基于深度学习的方法的最先进 (SOTA) 代码预测结果。然而，大多数之前基于深度学习的 SOTA 工作在提供预测代码的文本参考和解释方面仍处于早期阶段，尽管预测结果的这种可解释性水平对于获得专业医疗编码人员的信任至关重要。这就提出了一个重要问题：当前的可解释性方法如何应用于高级神经网络模型（例如 Transformer）来预测正确的代码并在支持代码预测的临床记录中提供参考。首先，我们提出了一个可解释的阅读、参加和编码 (xRAC) 框架，并通过使用基于 SOTA 变压器的模型 RAC 进行简化但彻底的人性化评估，评估两种方法：基于注意力评分的 xRAC-ATTN 和基于模型无关知识蒸馏的 xRAC-KD。我们发现 xRAC-ATTN 突出显示的支持证据文本质量高于 xRAC-KD，而 xRAC-KD 在生产部署场景中具有潜在优势。更重要的是，我们首次表明，考虑到可解释性方法的当前状态，使用 SOTA 医疗代码预测系统仍然需要专业编码员的专业知识和能力，尽管其预测精度优于人类编码员。我们相信，这是朝着开发可解释且准确的机器学习系统迈出的非常有意义的一步，以便根据临床记录进行完全自主的医疗代码预测。 本文评估当前可解释性方法在临床笔记中为医学编码预测提供参考文本的能力。 Byung-Hak Kim,Zhongfen Deng,Philip S. Yu,Varun Ganapathi cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CL
